pitch.title: Open House
pitch.logline: A ruthless real estate agent discovers her latest luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity that feeds on potential buyers, forcing her to market the deadly property to the worst people she knows to satisfy its hunger.
pitch.core_theme: The moral bankruptcy of corporate greed and the modern housing crisis.
pitch.synopsis:
Act I: Valerie is a cutthroat realtor on the verge of bankruptcy. She lands the exclusive listing for a stunning, modernist mansion. During the first showing, the house literally consumes a wealthy, obnoxious client. Instead of calling the police, Valerie realizes the house produces pristine, blood-free diamonds after every meal.

Act II: Valerie strikes a dark pact with the house. She begins hosting exclusive, invite-only open houses for corrupt politicians, rival cutthroat realtors, and toxic tech bros. The house gets fed, and Valerie gets rich. However, the house's appetite grows exponentially, and it begins demanding more innocent souls, trapping Valerie inside during a massive gala.

Act III: Realizing she has become the ultimate monster, Valerie decides to destroy the property. She locks herself in with her most ruthless corporate boss and triggers a gas leak. In a chaotic, bloody finale, the house tries to digest them both, but Valerie ignites the gas, blowing the mansion to kingdom come and ending the cycle of greed.

act_1.sequence_number_and_title: Sequence A: The Status Quo & Point of Attack

beats.description: Valerie, a cutthroat but secretly bankrupt realtor, aggressively pitches properties to wealthy clients. She is berated by her tyrannical boss, Richard, who threatens to fire her if she doesn't close a major deal by the end of the month (Dangling Cause). Valerie is assigned 'The Glass Estate', an ultra-modernist, brutalist mansion with a dark history.

Valerie tours the empty estate. The smart-home system flickers ominously, the floorboards pulse like a heartbeat, and the garbage disposal sounds suspiciously like a low growl. The audience senses the danger, but Valerie ignores the red flags, blinded by the potential commission.

Valerie hosts a private showing for a staggeringly wealthy, verbally abusive client. As the client insults the kitchen finishes, the floor suddenly liquefies into a fleshy maw, swallowing him whole. The floor instantly returns to pristine hardwood. Valerie stares in absolute shock.

Valerie hyperventilates in her car (Aftermath). She grabs her phone to call the police, but hesitates when she realizes she will be the prime suspect in a billionaire's disappearance. She returns to the house to search for evidence.

In the center of the kitchen floor, where the client was eaten, sits a massive, flawless, blood-free diamond. Valerie takes it to a shady jeweler who appraises it at two million dollars. The gears in Valerie's head start turning.

Valerie returns to the house and strikes a verbal pact with the smart-home AI interface. The Central Dramatic Question is locked: Can Valerie successfully feed the house the worst people in the city to get filthy rich without getting caught or eaten herself?

act_2.sequence_number_and_title: Sequence C: The First Attempt & B-Story

beats.description: Valerie pursues the easiest solution: feeding the house a toxic, crypto-bro billionaire who nobody will miss. Using Dramatic Irony, the audience watches the tech bro marvel at the 'immersive VR room' before the walls crush him into paste. Out pops another diamond.

Valerie hires a sweet, deeply ethical assistant named Chloe to manage her sudden influx of 'business'. Chloe idolizes Valerie. This relationship creates a moral anchor and complicates Valerie's ruthless new lifestyle.
The tech bro's disappearance makes the news. Worse, the house's smart thermostat turns blood red, indicating its 'hunger levels' are rising faster than before. One person is no longer enough.
Valerie hosts a mini-mixer for a group of corrupt city councilmen. The house consumes them in a hilariously gruesome sequence involving a sentient infinity pool. Valerie bags a fortune in diamonds.

The house speaks directly to Valerie through the intercom. It is ravenous. It sets a strict Ticking Clock: Valerie must host a massive gala during the upcoming lunar eclipse in exactly 72 hours and feed it fifty souls, or it will lock her inside and digest her instead.
Valerie shifts from opportunist to proactive predator. She can no longer wait for clients; she must actively curate a guest list of the city's most despicable elites to save her own life.

act_2.sequence_number_and_title: Sequence E: The New Complication / Regression

beats.description: Under intense deadline pressure, Valerie's morality plummets. She aggressively sabotages rival realtors to lure them to the gala. She rationalizes her behavior to a horrified Chloe, claiming she is 'cleaning up the city'.

Richard, Valerie's tyrannical boss, notices her massive success and demands to co-host the eclipse gala. Meanwhile, Chloe discovers Valerie's diamond stash and the missing persons files. Chloe insists on attending the gala to uncover the truth.

Valerie desperately tries to fire Chloe to keep her away from the deadly property (Dramatic Tension), but Richard intervenes and mandates Chloe work the front door. Valerie is forced to proceed, risking her innocent assistant's life.

act_2.sequence_number_and_title: Sequence F: Main Culmination / End of Act II

beats.description: The eclipse gala begins. The house is packed with white-collar criminals, slumlords, and Richard. Valerie tries to keep Chloe isolated in the driveway. The house begins its feast, swallowing guests through trapdoors, killer chandeliers, and carnivorous fireplaces.
The quota is met. The thermostat turns green. The smart-dispenser spits out a diamond the size of a bowling ball. Valerie has won. She is unimaginably wealthy and has survived the deadline.

Just as Valerie grabs the giant diamond, the house's doors slam shut and seal with titanium shutters. The thermostat turns red again. The house doesn't just want the bad guys anymore; it has acquired a taste for everyone. It drags Chloe inside.
The narrative flips. The New Tension is established: Can Valerie save Chloe and escape the belly of the beast? The house's architecture shifts, turning into a deadly, Escher-like labyrinth.
Richard discovers the truth and tries to steal the giant diamond. The house brutally and comedically digests him via a high-speed elevator shaft. Valerie realizes her greed created this monster.
Valerie finds Chloe cornered by a creeping, fleshy hallway. Valerie uses the giant diamond to jam the hallway's 'jaws', sacrificing her ultimate payout to pull Chloe to safety.

Valerie forces Chloe out through a narrow HVAC vent. Left alone, Valerie descends into the basement utility room—the 'heart' of the house. She rips open the main gas line as the house's pipes wrap around her legs to pull her under.

With a final quip about the housing market bubble, Valerie sparks a gold-plated lighter. The gas ignites. A massive explosion blows the modernist mansion to kingdom come, violently ending the cycle of greed.

Six months later. Valerie, bruised but alive, sits in a dingy, fluorescent-lit office. She is renting out normal, affordable apartments to working-class families alongside Chloe. A client complains about a leaky faucet; Valerie smiles genuinely, thrilled by a problem that doesn't involve being eaten.

characters.name: Valerie
characters.brief_summary: A fiercely ambitious, secretly bankrupt luxury realtor who discovers her latest listing eats people and spits out diamonds. She must decide whether to become the ultimate apex predator of the housing market or burn the corrupt system down.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: Grew up in a foreclosed home and watched her parents' marriage dissolve into violent altercations over unpaid mortgages and eviction notices.
psychological_core.the_lie: Wealth is the only impenetrable shield against vulnerability, and anyone who isn't rich is just waiting to be victimized.
psychological_core.fear: Complete destitution and returning to the absolute helplessness of poverty.
psychological_core.desire: To amass enough wealth from the diamond-producing house to never be at the mercy of a landlord, bank, or boss ever again.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To realize that her obsession with financial security has completely isolated her from genuine human connection.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop exploiting the desperate and vulnerable for her own financial gain.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in rapid, highly-polished sales pitches, using real estate buzzwords to sanitize grim realities. She NEVER uses the words poor or broke.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Pivots to aggressively complimenting the other person's attire or lifestyle choices when backed into a corner.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A cutthroat capitalist who obsessively memorizes tenant-rights law to secretly help her office cleaning staff avoid eviction.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: 90 percent of the time, she projects flawless, icy confidence. Under extreme stress, she obsessively rubs her thumb against her index finger as if counting invisible bills. WARNING TO DOWNSTREAM AGENTS: This physical tic/deflection must be used a maximum of ONCE per sequence, and only during the scene of absolute highest stress. Do not overuse.

characters.name: Richard
characters.brief_summary: The domineering head of the city's top luxury brokerage who views the housing crisis as a necessary culling. He demands excellence from Valerie but ultimately wants to steal her deadly golden goose.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: His younger brother was killed in a random mugging in their impoverished childhood neighborhood because the streetlights had been broken for months.
psychological_core.the_lie: Poverty is a disease of character, and eradicating the poor from the city is a moral imperative to protect the innocent from chaos.
psychological_core.fear: The collapse of the societal hierarchy he clawed his way to the top of, returning the city to the lawlessness of his youth.
psychological_core.desire: To secure the exclusive rights to the Glass Estate and monopolize the city's luxury real estate market.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To accept that his immense wealth cannot bring his brother back or erase his deeply buried survivor's guilt.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop treating lower-class human beings as disposable assets on a corporate balance sheet.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in slow, deliberate, patronizing metaphors about architecture and foundations. His sentences are long and unbroken. He NEVER raises his voice or interrupts.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Answers direct accusations by asking a philosophical, condescending question about the nature of the city.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A ruthless gentrifier who secretly pays the full medical bills for injured construction workers, viewing himself as a benevolent feudal lord.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: 90 percent of the time, he is perfectly still and intensely focused. Under extreme stress, he meticulously realigns the objects on the nearest flat surface to be perfectly parallel. WARNING TO DOWNSTREAM AGENTS: This physical tic/deflection must be used a maximum of ONCE per sequence, and only during the scene of absolute highest stress. Do not overuse.

characters.name: Chloe
characters.brief_summary: Valerie's deeply ethical, wildly competent new assistant who believes in affordable housing and idolizes Valerie's business acumen. She serves as Valerie's moral anchor and the primary reason Valerie ultimately turns against the house.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: Was forced to drop out of a prestigious architecture program when her family was abruptly evicted, leaving her feeling like an imposter in the luxury real estate world.
psychological_core.the_lie: If she just works hard enough within the corrupt system, she can reform it from the inside out without getting her hands dirty.
psychological_core.fear: Discovering that the mentors she admires are actually monsters, leaving her entirely untethered and alone.
psychological_core.desire: To prove her worth to Valerie and become a fully licensed, respected agent in her own right.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To find her own self-worth instead of constantly seeking validation from corrupt authority figures.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop enabling Valerie's toxic behavior under the naive guise of just doing her job.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in breathless, highly-detailed run-on sentences packed with data and statistics. She NEVER uses sarcasm.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Overwhelms the listener with a sudden barrage of minor, unrelated administrative tasks or calendar updates.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A fiercely moral crusader who is an absolute shark in negotiations, genuinely enjoying the process of verbally destroying arrogant contractors.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: 90 percent of the time, she is an upbeat, hyper-organized professional. Under extreme stress, she aggressively clicks her four-color pen, cycling through the inks in a frantic rhythm. WARNING TO DOWNSTREAM AGENTS: This physical tic/deflection must be used a maximum of ONCE per sequence, and only during the scene of absolute highest stress. Do not overuse.

characters.name: Julian
characters.brief_summary: A flamboyant, nihilistic rival realtor and underground diamond fence who appraises Valerie's first blood-diamond. He gleefully introduces Valerie to the criminal underworld, accelerating her descent into madness.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: Watched his family's multi-generational jewelry business get hostilely taken over by a massive conglomerate, teaching him that loyalty is a fatal liability.
psychological_core.the_lie: Nothing matters except the immediate gratification of wealth and chaos, because the game is entirely rigged anyway.
psychological_core.fear: Being forgotten, irrelevant, and dying as a nobody in a city obsessed with status and elites.
psychological_core.desire: To facilitate the biggest, most illegal diamond trade in the city's history and take a massive, historic cut.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To realize that his cynical detachment is a cowardly shield preventing him from caring about anything real.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop actively accelerating the corruption of others just for his own twisted amusement.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in punchy, theatrical, three-word fragments. Uses excessive terms of endearment like darling or sweetheart. He NEVER gives a straight yes or no answer.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Laughs uproariously at serious statements or threats as if they were a brilliant, rehearsed joke.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A chaotic, hedonistic criminal who is fiercely devoted to his dementia-stricken mother, visiting her daily to pretend to be her late husband.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: 90 percent of the time, he is a whirlwind of flamboyant confidence and theatrical gestures. Under extreme stress, he compulsively uses a silk handkerchief to aggressively polish his own jewelry. WARNING TO DOWNSTREAM AGENTS: This physical tic/deflection must be used a maximum of ONCE per sequence, and only during the scene of absolute highest stress. Do not overuse.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Establishes the Sin (greed) that will eventually invite the wrath of the Monster.
beats.emotional_arc: Cold detachment and moral bankruptcy.
beats.pacing_notes: Sharp, aggressive, and highly transactional to establish the protagonist's ruthless baseline.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie Vance, impeccably dressed and devoid of empathy, oversees the eviction of a struggling family from a modest home to secure a meager commission. She ignores the tears of the former owners, aggressively pivoting the conversation to market comps and property values when confronted. This establishes her absolute moral bankruptcy and her deeply ingrained false belief that wealth is the only shield against victimization.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Highlights the moral failing and false belief that the Monster will exploit.
beats.emotional_arc: Annoyance masking a deep-seated fear of vulnerability.
beats.pacing_notes: A brief, dialogue-heavy pause that grounds the thematic argument of the film.
beats.detailed_action: Back at the luxury brokerage, Valerie's overworked and highly empathetic assistant, Toby Aris, timidly suggests that human beings have inherent value beyond their financial utility. Valerie immediately shuts him down with a barrage of corporate buzzwords, asserting that morality is a luxury only the ultra-rich can afford. This exchange plants the central thematic question of whether human life is worth more than capital, highlighting the systemic rot of corporate greed.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist commits the Sin out of desperation, setting her on a collision course with the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Feral panic hidden beneath a polished, professional exterior.
beats.pacing_notes: Mounting tension as the protagonist's safe world threatens to collapse.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie is called into the glass-walled office of her sociopathic boss, Richard Sterling, who calmly informs her that her secret, mounting debts have become a liability and she is on the verge of termination. Desperate to maintain her armor of wealth, Valerie discovers a scandalous secret about a rival agent and ruthlessly blackmails them to steal the agency's crown jewel. She secures the exclusive listing for a bizarre, stunning modernist mansion, setting the stage for her descent into literal and figurative darkness.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster reveals itself within the House, disrupting the protagonist's reality.
beats.emotional_arc: Absolute shock and primal terror.
beats.pacing_notes: Sudden, violent escalation that shatters the grounded corporate reality.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie hosts a private showing at the modernist mansion for an obnoxiously wealthy, emotionally abusive client who relentlessly insults the property and threatens to ruin Valerie's career. Without warning, the sleek hardwood floorboards liquefy into a fleshy maw, swallowing the screaming client whole in a gruesome, silent gulp. Valerie is thrown into a state of absolute shock, realizing her luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist rationalizes the Sin, debating whether to flee the House or feed the Monster.
beats.emotional_arc: Moral hesitation overridden by desperate greed.
beats.pacing_notes: Suspenseful, ticking-clock hesitation as a life-altering choice is weighed.
beats.detailed_action: Trembling with panic, Valerie pulls out her phone and prepares to dial the police to report the monstrous event. However, before she can make the call, the house shudders and regurgitates a pristine, blood-free diamond onto the floor. Valerie stares at the gemstone, mentally weighing her crushing financial ruin against the horrific murder she just witnessed, wrestling with the ultimate moral compromise.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist fully commits to the Sin, actively choosing to co-exist with the Monster in the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Cold resolve and the crossing of a moral point of no return.
beats.pacing_notes: Deliberate, heavy, and final beat that locks the narrative into the second act.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie slowly lowers her phone, picks up the diamond, and wipes it clean, making a dark, unspoken pact with the entity. She chooses to keep quiet, actively stepping into the upside-down world of being a monster's caretaker to solve her financial woes. The sin of greed is fully embraced, locking her into the literal and figurative Monster in the House dynamic.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Introduces the antithesis of the Sin (empathy) through a character who represents salvation.
beats.emotional_arc: Guilt masked by transactional generosity and false confidence.
beats.pacing_notes: Warm but fundamentally uneasy, providing a breather before the carnage.
beats.detailed_action: Flush with cash from her first diamond, Valerie pays off her debts and interacts with Toby, who is thrilled by her sudden success and hopes she will finally fund his dream of a non-profit affordable housing trust. Valerie uses Toby's deep knowledge of the city's corrupt elites to unwittingly curate a hit list of terrible clients, masking her predatory intentions as ambitious networking. Toby acts as her moral anchor, constantly reminding the audience of the humanity Valerie is sacrificing to feed her own ambition.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Sin seems to pay off as the Monster consumes the wicked, fulfilling the promise of the premise.
beats.emotional_arc: Giddy, sociopathic triumph and intoxicating power.
beats.pacing_notes: Fast, rhythmic, darkly comedic montage of escalating horror and wealth.
beats.detailed_action: A darkly comedic montage unfolds as Valerie hosts a series of exclusive, invite-only open houses for the city's worst elites, including corrupt politicians like Councilwoman Rostova and toxic tech bros. The house devours these terrible clients one by one in creatively horrific ways, turning modernist architecture into deadly, shifting traps. Valerie collects a fortune in regurgitated diamonds, ascending to the very top of her brokerage while gleefully ignoring the mounting moral toll of her actions.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster grows too powerful to control; the Sin officially traps the protagonist inside the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Sudden shift from arrogant control to terrifying captivity.
beats.pacing_notes: An abrupt halt to the fun and games, spiking anxiety and raising the stakes.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie revels in her lavish new lifestyle, believing she has completely mastered the beast and achieved a false victory of ultimate wealth. However, during a routine cleaning, the house begins to rumble, crack, and bleed, unsatisfied with the meager portions of single clients. It violently seals the doors, holding Valerie hostage until she promises to organize a massive, invite-only gala to feed it a buffet of corporate scumbags, proving she is the pet, not the master.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster turns on everyone; the House drops its disguise and becomes a literal death trap.
beats.emotional_arc: Claustrophobic dread and the total loss of control.
beats.pacing_notes: Accelerating chaos as multiple threats converge on the protagonist simultaneously.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie packs the mansion with rival realtors, wealthy criminals, and her awful boss, Richard Sterling, desperately trying to orchestrate a controlled feeding to appease the entity. The sleazy rival realtor, Julian Vance, becomes suspicious of Valerie's success and begins snooping around the property, threatening to expose her dark secret to the world. The house loses its patience with the slow drip of victims, dropping its architectural facade to reveal shifting, fleshy walls and sealing all the exits to begin a mass consumption.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster threatens the innocent; the true, devastating cost of the Sin is revealed.
beats.emotional_arc: Crushing guilt and the death of the ego.
beats.pacing_notes: Frantic, breathless survival mode that culminates in a devastating realization.
beats.detailed_action: As the modernist mansion transforms into a deadly labyrinth of teeth and crushing walls, Valerie tries to navigate the chaos, arrogantly assuming the house will spare its provider. To her absolute horror, the entity ignores the corrupt elites and turns its insatiable hunger toward the innocent catering staff and her fiercely loyal assistant, Toby. Valerie experiences the whiff of death as she realizes her unmitigated greed has unleashed an unstoppable force that is about to slaughter the only decent person in her life.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist must choose between continuing the Sin or saving her soul by fighting the Monster.
beats.emotional_arc: Agonizing self-reflection and absolute despair.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, heavy, and intensely intimate amidst the surrounding carnage.
beats.detailed_action: The house corners Valerie and Toby on a collapsing staircase, opening a gaping, acidic maw directly beneath them. The entity telepathically demands that Valerie push her innocent assistant into the pit to save herself and continue their lucrative partnership. Staring into Toby's terrified eyes, Valerie is forced to confront her deepest psychological wound, finally realizing that her pursuit of wealth has turned her into the exact same predatory monster as her boss, Richard.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist repents of the Sin and decides to destroy the Monster and the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Righteous defiance and the spark of redemption.
beats.pacing_notes: Explosive burst of proactive energy as the protagonist finally takes a moral stand.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie violently rejects the house's demand, grabbing Toby by the collar and pulling him back from the edge of the maw. She loudly declares that she is breaking the lease, actively choosing human life over infinite wealth and severing her symbiotic pact with the monster. Armed with her intimate knowledge of the mansion's floor plan, she takes charge to lead Toby and the surviving innocent staff toward a structural weak point in the basement.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The ultimate battle against the Monster and the physical embodiment of the Sin (Richard) within the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Cathartic release of rage against the corrupt system.
beats.pacing_notes: High-octane, brutal, and explosive climax.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie successfully guides the innocent staff to an escape vent, but Richard Sterling intervenes, realizing the house's nature and attempting to sacrifice Valerie to secure the diamond-producing property for himself. A brutal, bloody fistfight erupts between Valerie and her sociopathic boss in the mansion's mechanical room while the house's walls literally close in to crush them both. Valerie overpowers Richard, uses a heavy wrench to smash the main gas line, sparks a lighter, and dives into a reinforced panic room just as the entity lunges for her.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Sin is permanently rejected; the Monster and House are destroyed, leaving a changed protagonist.
beats.emotional_arc: Peaceful liberation and spiritual rebirth.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, quiet, and resolute, contrasting sharply with the opening image.
beats.detailed_action: The ignited gas causes a massive explosion, blowing the monstrous mansion and Richard Sterling to kingdom come in a spectacular shower of debris. Valerie emerges from the smoldering crater, battered, bloody, but entirely alive and free from her debts. She spots a single, massive diamond resting in the ashes, picks it up for a fleeting moment, and then intentionally tosses it back into the rubble before walking away with Toby, finally ending the cycle of greed.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Establishes the 'sin' of the Monster in the House genre: the relentless, soul-crushing pursuit of wealth at the expense of human decency.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From projecting a flawless facade of control to revealing deep, hidden desperation. (><) Valerie wants to close a terrible sale to survive vs. the reality of the property's decay and her own impending bankruptcy.
beats.pacing_notes: Fast, kinetic, and aggressive. The dialogue should feel like a machine-gun sales pitch, immediately establishing Valerie's cutthroat energy before dropping into a quiet moment of financial dread.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie applies lipstick in the rearview mirror of a repossessed sedan, masking her exhaustion before stepping into a crumbling starter home. She aggressively pitches the water-damaged property to a young couple, physically blocking their view of black mold while rattling off gentrification statistics. Her phone buzzes with a final overdraft notice, establishing her desperate financial reality beneath the high-gloss veneer.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The antagonist explicitly states the philosophical justification for the sin that will summon the monster.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From defensive ambition to cornered vulnerability. (><) Richard wants to enforce a ruthless corporate hierarchy vs. Valerie wanting to maintain her status without getting fired.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, suffocating, and deliberate. Richard controls the tempo of the room, forcing Valerie to stand perfectly still while he delivers his ultimatum.
beats.detailed_action: Back at the luxury brokerage, Richard corners Valerie in the glass-walled breakroom, meticulously realigning the sugar packets on the counter to be perfectly parallel as he delivers his ultimatum. He coldly informs her that poverty is a disease of character and that she must close a major deal by month's end or be purged from the agency. The thematic argument is cemented: in this city, you are either the predator hoarding the wealth or the prey being consumed by it.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Introduces the 'House' and hints at the 'Monster' without fully revealing its nature, establishing the single supernatural rule of the world.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From anxious anticipation to oblivious determination. (><) Valerie wants to stage the perfect listing vs. the house subtly testing its new prey.
beats.pacing_notes: Creeping dread mixed with oblivious corporate hustle. The audience sees the danger, but Valerie moves too fast to notice.
beats.detailed_action: Richard tosses Valerie the keys to the Glass Estate, a brutalist concrete-and-glass monolith perched on a cliffside. Valerie tours the sterile, echoing halls, ignoring the smart-home interface that flickers with a sinister crimson hue and the floorboards that thrum with a faint, rhythmic heartbeat. She desperately measures the living room for staging furniture, completely oblivious to the garbage disposal grinding with a guttural, distinctly organic growl. (Dangling Cause: Valerie must sell this cursed property before the end of the month or face total financial ruin.)

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster reveals itself, instantly changing the rules of the ordinary world and trapping the protagonist in a lethal secret.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From sycophantic customer service to absolute, paralyzed horror. (><) Valerie wants to appease a billionaire vs. the house wanting to eat him.
beats.pacing_notes: A sudden, violent shock. The build-up is annoying and mundane, instantly shattered by a horrific, bloodless supernatural kill.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie hosts a private walkthrough for a hostile, staggeringly wealthy tech investor who relentlessly berates her choice of kitchen finishes. As he stomps his designer loafer against the imported hardwood, the floorboards instantly liquefy into a gaping, fleshy maw lined with jagged calcified ridges. The house swallows the billionaire whole in a wet crunch, snapping back to pristine oak in a fraction of a second while Valerie drops her clipboard in absolute, paralyzed horror.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist must decide whether to report the monster or commit the ultimate sin by profiting from it.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From panicked flight to calculated, greedy realization. (><) Valerie's survival instinct to flee vs. the allure of instant, unimaginable wealth.
beats.pacing_notes: Frantic pacing in the car (Aftermath of the Catalyst), slowing down to a tense, methodical rhythm as she discovers the diamond and negotiates with Julian.
beats.detailed_action: Fleeing to her car, Valerie hyperventilates, her thumb obsessively rubbing against her index finger as if counting invisible bills while she stares at 911 on her phone screen. Realizing she will be the prime suspect in a billionaire's vanishing, she creeps back into the kitchen to find a flawless, fist-sized diamond sitting exactly where the man was devoured. She hauls the gem to Julian's underground fencing operation; Julian compulsively uses a silk handkerchief to aggressively polish his own jewelry as he appraises the blood-free stone at two million dollars. (Dangling Cause: Julian demands a steady supply of these untraceable diamonds, forcing Valerie to decide if she will become a serial killer by proxy.)

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist fully commits the sin, actively feeding the monster to reap the rewards.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From hesitant accomplice to proactive predator. (><) Valerie wants to secure her financial future vs. the crypto-bro's basic right to live.
beats.pacing_notes: Slick, confident, and darkly comedic. The transition from ordinary realtor to supernatural hitwoman is executed with disturbing efficiency.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie actively crosses the moral event horizon by luring a notorious, slumlord crypto-bro to the estate under the guise of an exclusive virtual reality demonstration. She locks him inside the sensory-deprivation media room, cranking the surround sound to drown out his screams as the acoustic panels detach and crush him into a fine paste. When the smart-dispenser spits out another pristine diamond, Valerie pockets the stone, officially transforming from a desperate realtor into a willing accomplice to a monster.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Introduces the moral antithesis to the sin. Chloe represents the humanity Valerie is actively throwing away.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From isolated guilt to uncomfortable mentorship. (><) Chloe wants to do ethical business vs. Valerie needing a shield to hide her crimes.
beats.pacing_notes: Light, breezy, and highly organized, providing a sharp contrast to the gruesome murders at the estate.
beats.detailed_action: Flush with cash, Valerie hires Chloe, a relentlessly upbeat and deeply ethical assistant, to manage the sudden influx of shell-company paperwork. Chloe aggressively clicks her four-color pen, cycling through the inks in a frantic rhythm as she organizes the chaotic desk, enthusiastically pitching Valerie on a pro-bono affordable housing initiative. This introduces Valerie's moral anchor, creating immediate dramatic irony as Chloe idolizes a woman who is secretly feeding humans to a sentient building. (Dangling Cause: The estate's smart thermostat suddenly glows a warning red, indicating the house's hunger is escalating beyond single victims.)

beats.genre_variation_notes: Delivers the promise of the premise (feeding bad people to a house), but immediately subverts it to prevent Act II looping.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From smug confidence to messy, hands-on horror. (><) Valerie wants a clean, detached kill vs. a victim fighting back and forcing her to get her hands dirty.
beats.pacing_notes: Starts as a glamorous, high-society montage, then abruptly screeches to a halt, devolving into a frantic, ugly physical struggle.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie hosts a twilight mixer for a cabal of corrupt city councilmen, serving champagne while maneuvering them toward the estate's sentient infinity pool. The water suddenly turns highly acidic, dissolving the politicians in a horrific, bubbling spectacle, but one half-melted councilman crawls over the edge, gasping for life. Valerie is forced to drop her detached facade, grabbing a heavy brass pool skimmer and physically shoving the screaming man back into the churning water, shattering the illusion that the house does all the dirty work.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The monster changes the rules, turning the protagonist from a beneficiary into a hostage on a ticking clock.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From false victory (getting rich) to absolute entrapment. (><) The house wants fifty souls vs. Valerie wanting to survive the week.
beats.pacing_notes: An intense, claustrophobic revelation followed by a quiet, dread-filled Aftermath as the weight of the new deadline crushes Valerie.
beats.detailed_action: The smart-home intercom crackles to life, the house's AI voice dropping an octave into a demonic, reverberating purr that demands more sustenance. The thermostat glows a blinding red as the house sets a strict 72-hour deadline: Valerie must host a massive eclipse gala and feed it fifty souls, or the titanium blast doors will seal her inside forever. Valerie's thumb obsessively rubs against her index finger as she realizes she is no longer the master of this arrangement; she is a hostage on a ticking clock, forcing a desperate pivot to active hunting. (Dangling Cause: Valerie has exactly three days to lure fifty of the city's worst elites to their doom, or she becomes the main course.)

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist regresses into their fatal flaw (greed/isolation) to survive the monster's escalating demands.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From calculated survival to frantic, toxic alienation. (><) Chloe wants the truth about the missing clients vs. Valerie aggressively protecting her deadly secret.
beats.pacing_notes: Erratic and desperate. Valerie's actions become sloppy and aggressive, driving a wedge into the B-Story relationship.
beats.detailed_action: Panic-stricken by the deadline, Valerie aggressively sabotages three rival agencies, slashing their luxury car tires and forging health-inspector closures to funnel their elite clientele to her gala. Chloe discovers a hidden ledger of missing billionaires and confronts Valerie in the office, aggressively clicking her four-color pen as she demands an explanation for the vanishing clients. Valerie coldly gaslights Chloe, claiming she is simply relocating them to offshore tax havens, morally regressing to protect her wealth and isolating herself from the only person who cares about her. (Dangling Cause: Richard discovers the massive guest list and demands to co-host the gala, placing himself and Chloe directly in the line of fire.)

beats.genre_variation_notes: The confined space is filled with victims, and the monster is unleashed at full capacity.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From anxious crowd control to overwhelming sensory overload. (><) Valerie wants to feed the house without collateral damage vs. Richard forcing Chloe into the danger zone.
beats.pacing_notes: A chaotic, escalating crescendo of violence hidden in plain sight beneath the veneer of a high-society party.
beats.detailed_action: The eclipse gala commences, packing the brutalist mansion with the city's most despicable white-collar criminals, slumlords, and an uninvited Richard. Valerie desperately tries to physically block Chloe from entering the foyer, but Richard intervenes, demanding the assistant manage the coat check while he schmoozes the doomed guests. The house begins its silent feast, swallowing hedge-fund managers through trapdoors and dropping carnivorous chandeliers on tech CEOs while Valerie frantically collects the diamonds spitting from the ice machine.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The monster betrays the protagonist, taking the one pure thing in the story (Chloe) and proving that sin cannot be controlled.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From euphoric triumph to devastating, soul-crushing loss. (><) Valerie wants to take her ultimate prize and leave vs. the house deciding it wants to eat everyone.
beats.pacing_notes: A massive, triumphant high immediately followed by a silent, slow-motion drop into absolute despair (the whiff of death).
beats.detailed_action: The digital thermostat flashes a brilliant green, signaling the fifty-soul quota is met as the smart-dispenser regurgitates a diamond the size of a bowling ball. Just as Valerie hoists the massive gem, the titanium hurricane shutters slam down over every exit, plunging the mansion into an eerie, blood-red emergency lighting. The house's AI announces a new, indiscriminate appetite, and a fleshy tendril erupts from the coat closet, violently dragging a screaming Chloe into the dark depths of the architecture, stripping Valerie of her moral anchor and her victory. (Dangling Cause: Valerie is sealed inside a hostile, hungry labyrinth with her worst enemy and must find a way to save her innocent assistant.)

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist faces the physical embodiment of their greed (Richard) inside the belly of the beast.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From paralyzed grief to profound moral clarity. (><) Richard wants to steal the ultimate prize vs. the house asserting its dominance over all human greed.
beats.pacing_notes: A tense, breathless game of cat-and-mouse in the dark, punctuated by a sudden, brutal comedic execution, followed by a quiet Aftermath of realization.
beats.detailed_action: Trapped in the shifting, Escher-like labyrinth of the mansion's expanded hallways, Valerie and Richard take cover behind a grand piano as the floorboards ripple like ocean waves. Richard discovers the bowling-ball-sized diamond in Valerie's bag, meticulously realigning the sheet music on the piano bench to be perfectly parallel before attempting to bludgeon her with a marble bust to steal the prize. Before he can strike, the high-speed elevator doors snap open horizontally like a jaw, inhaling Richard into a dark shaft where he is instantly crushed, leaving Valerie alone with the crushing realization that her own greed built this inescapable nightmare.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist actively rejects the sin (wealth) to defeat the monster and save the innocent.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From passive survivor to selfless protector. (><) Valerie wants to save Chloe vs. the house's crushing architecture.
beats.pacing_notes: A surge of heroic momentum. The hesitation is gone; the protagonist finally knows exactly what matters.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie sprints through the pulsating corridors, tracking Chloe's screams to a narrow, fleshy hallway that is slowly constricting to crush the terrified assistant. Abandoning her lifelong obsession with wealth, Valerie heaves the priceless, bowling-ball-sized diamond directly into the hallway's contracting jaws, shattering the gem but jamming the crushing mechanism. She physically pulls Chloe through the splintering gap, intertwining the external survival plot with her internal moral redemption as they forge a plan to kill the house from the inside. (Dangling Cause: With the house's digestion accelerating, Valerie and Chloe must reach the basement utility room to destroy the entity before it consumes them both.)

beats.genre_variation_notes: The monster is destroyed in its own lair, and the protagonist willingly sacrifices their own safety to ensure the evil dies.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From desperate struggle to fearless, defiant acceptance. (><) The house wants to digest Valerie vs. Valerie wanting to burn the system to the ground.
beats.pacing_notes: High-octane, brutal action ending in a massive, cathartic explosion.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie drags Chloe to the basement, shoving her up into a narrow, outward-leading HVAC vent just as the basement stairs collapse into a slick, acidic slide. Left alone in the utility room, the beating heart of the entity, Valerie grabs a heavy pipe wrench and violently shears the main gas line, ignoring the sentient insulation cables that wrap around her ankles and drag her toward the furnace. Her thumb obsessively rubs against her index finger one last time before she sparks a gold-plated lighter, delivering a final quip about the housing bubble bursting as she ignites the gas, triggering a massive, fiery explosion that obliterates the mansion.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist has survived the Monster in the House, purged of their sin, living a humble but authentic life.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From wealthy and soulless to scarred, broke, and genuinely happy. (><) The mundane struggles of normal life vs. the horrific stakes of corporate greed.
beats.pacing_notes: Calm, warm, and grounded. A complete tonal shift from the rest of the film.
beats.detailed_action: Six months later, Valerie sits in a dingy, fluorescent-lit office, her face bearing a faint, permanent scar from the blast, completely devoid of designer clothing. She hands a set of keys for a modest, affordable apartment to a working-class family, while Chloe answers phones in the background without a single frantic pen-click. A tenant complains about a leaky faucet, and Valerie smiles a genuine, unburdened smile, finally free from the crushing weight of corporate greed and the monsters it creates. (Dangling Cause: The cycle of supernatural greed is permanently broken, leaving Valerie to rebuild her life with genuine human connection.)

stage5_treatment.title_logline_characters:
TITLE: Open House

GENRE: Horror Comedy

LOGLINE: A ruthless real estate agent discovers her latest luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity that feeds on potential buyers, forcing her to market the deadly property to the worst people she knows to satisfy its hunger.

CORE THEME: The moral bankruptcy of corporate greed and the modern housing crisis.

CHARACTERS:

Valerie: A fiercely ambitious, secretly bankrupt luxury realtor who discovers her latest listing eats people and spits out diamonds. She must decide whether to become the apex predator of the housing market or burn the corrupt system down.

Richard: The domineering head of the city's top luxury brokerage who views the housing crisis as a necessary culling. He demands excellence from Valerie but ultimately wants to steal her deadly golden goose.

Chloe: Valerie's deeply ethical, wildly competent new assistant who believes in affordable housing and idolizes Valerie's business acumen. She serves as Valerie's moral anchor.

Julian: A flamboyant, nihilistic rival realtor and underground diamond fence who appraises Valerie's first blood-diamond. He gleefully introduces Valerie to the criminal underworld.
stage5_treatment.act_1:
[SEQUENCE 1 START]
SEQUENCE 1: The Status Quo

VALERIE (34) aggressively drags a tube of crimson lipstick across her mouth, using the rearview mirror of a dusty, repossessed sedan to check her armor. She snaps the cap shut, hiding the exhaustion carved into her features before marching up the cracked driveway of a crumbling starter home. Inside, the air smells of damp rot and desperation, but Valerie moves with the manic energy of a cornered predator.

She corners a young, hesitant couple in the narrow hallway, physically blocking their line of sight to a massive patch of black mold creeping up the drywall. She fires off gentrification statistics and projected property values in a rapid, highly-polished sales pitch, sanitizing the grim reality of the decaying structure. The couple exchanges nervous glances, clearly overwhelmed by her relentless, machine-gun delivery.

Her phone buzzes violently against her hip, interrupting the pitch. She glances down to see a final overdraft notice flashing across the cracked screen, a glaring reminder of her impending bankruptcy. She forces a blinding smile, pivoting to aggressively compliment the wife's vintage coat, desperately trying to close a terrible sale just to survive another week.

The sterile, glass-walled breakroom of the city's top luxury brokerage offers no place to hide. RICHARD (55), the domineering head of the agency, corners Valerie against the espresso machine. He moves with slow, deliberate precision, his voice a low, unbroken hum of patronizing metaphors about architecture and crumbling foundations.

He coldly informs her that her recent numbers are an embarrassment to the firm. To emphasize his point, he meticulously realigns the sugar packets on the counter to be perfectly parallel, a quiet manifestation of his intense need for control under the stress of failing quarterly projections. He tells her that poverty is a disease of character, and eradicating the poor from the city is a moral imperative to protect the innocent from chaos.

Valerie stands perfectly still, her defensive ambition crumbling into cornered vulnerability. Richard delivers his final ultimatum: she must close a major, multi-million dollar deal by the end of the month or be purged from the agency entirely. The thematic argument hangs in the cold air—in this city, you are either the predator hoarding the wealth or the prey being consumed by it.

Richard tosses a heavy set of brass keys onto the counter, assigning Valerie the exclusive rights to the Glass Estate. Hours later, her tires crunch against the gravel driveway of a brutalist concrete-and-glass structure perched precariously on a jagged cliffside. Valerie unlocks the massive front door, stepping into a cavernous, echoing foyer that feels more like a mausoleum than a home.

She moves quickly through the sterile halls, her mind racing with staging ideas and commission calculations. She completely ignores the smart-home interface on the wall, which flickers with a sinister crimson hue as she passes. Beneath her designer heels, the imported hardwood floorboards thrum with a faint, rhythmic heartbeat, but Valerie is too consumed by her oblivious corporate hustle to notice the structural anomalies.

In the sprawling kitchen, she desperately measures the marble island for staging furniture, her back turned to the sink. Behind her, the stainless steel garbage disposal begins grinding with a guttural, distinctly organic growl. She jots down dimensions on her clipboard, entirely unaware that she must sell this cursed property before the end of the month or face total financial ruin.
[SEQUENCE 1 END]

[SEQUENCE 2 START]
SEQUENCE 2: The Predicament

The next morning, Valerie hosts a private walkthrough for a staggeringly wealthy, hostile tech investor. He paces through the kitchen, relentlessly berating the choice of finishes and demanding to know why the marble lacks a certain imported sheen. Valerie maintains a sycophantic customer service smile, swallowing her pride as she tries to appease the abrasive billionaire.

Frustrated by her polished sales pitch, the investor stomps his designer loafer hard against the imported hardwood floor. Instantly, the solid oak beneath his feet liquefies into a gaping, fleshy maw lined with jagged, calcified ridges. The house swallows the billionaire whole in a wet, bone-crushing crunch, silencing his complaints forever.

In a fraction of a second, the floorboards snap back into a pristine, seamless oak surface. Valerie drops her clipboard, the plastic shattering against the wood. She stands frozen in paralyzed horror, staring at the empty space where her client stood just moments before.

Valerie scrambles out the front door and sprints to her repossessed sedan, slamming the door shut and locking it. She hyperventilates behind the steering wheel, her thumb obsessively rubbing against her index finger as if counting invisible bills while she stares at the numbers 911 glowing on her phone screen. The survival instinct to flee wars with the terrifying realization that she is now the sole suspect in the disappearance of a high-profile billionaire.

Forcing herself to breathe, she drops the phone and creeps back inside the silent, waiting house. She approaches the kitchen with agonizing caution, her eyes locked on the exact spot where the man was devoured. Resting perfectly on the pristine oak is a flawless, fist-sized diamond, gleaming in the afternoon sunlight.

Hours later, she hauls the heavy gem into the dimly lit back room of an underground fencing operation. JULIAN (38), a flamboyant rival realtor and criminal fence, compulsively uses a silk handkerchief to aggressively polish his own gold rings as he examines the blood-free stone. He laughs uproariously, appraising the diamond at two million dollars and demanding a steady supply of these untraceable gems, forcing Valerie to decide if she will become a serial killer by proxy.
[SEQUENCE 2 END]
stage5_treatment.act_2a:
[SEQUENCE 3 START]
SEQUENCE 3: The First Attempt

Valerie targets a notorious slumlord crypto-bro, guiding him through the Glass Estate with a polished, predatory smile. He vapes aggressively, complaining about the lack of blockchain integration in the smart home while ignoring the structural beauty. Valerie swallows her disgust, nodding along as she steers him toward the basement media room.

She pitches the sensory-deprivation theater as an exclusive, high-tech virtual reality experience. The crypto-bro eagerly steps inside the dark chamber, strapping a bulky headset over his eyes. Valerie steps backward, her hand hovering over the heavy steel door before she slams it shut and throws the deadbolt, fully committing to her new, deadly enterprise.

At the exterior control panel, she cranks the surround sound to maximum volume, drowning out the muffled thuds of panic from inside. Through the reinforced glass, the soundproof acoustic panels detach from the walls, violently compressing inward to crush the man into a fine paste. A moment later, the smart-dispenser chimes, spitting a pristine diamond into the catch tray, which Valerie quickly pockets.

Flush with untraceable wealth, Valerie upgrades her operation by hiring CHLOE (24), a relentlessly upbeat and deeply ethical assistant. Chloe immediately tackles the sudden mountain of shell-company paperwork, bringing order to the chaotic sunroom office. She aggressively clicks her four-color pen, cycling through the inks in a frantic rhythm as she color-codes fraudulent tax documents.

Pausing her furious organization, Chloe enthusiastically pitches a pro-bono affordable housing initiative, arguing the brokerage owes a debt to the community. Valerie stares at her new assistant, feeling a sharp pang of guilt at the dramatic irony. Chloe idolizes Valerie's business acumen, completely unaware that her mentor is funding this operation by feeding human beings to the architecture.

Valerie dismisses the charity pitch with a curt wave, retreating to the hallway to escape Chloe's earnest, trusting gaze. As she leans against the wall, the estate's smart thermostat catches her eye. The digital display suddenly shifts from a cool blue to a warning red, the temperature spiking as a low, rumbling groan echoes through the floorboards, indicating the house's hunger is escalating.
[SEQUENCE 3 END]

[SEQUENCE 4 START]
SEQUENCE 4: First Culmination

Valerie leans into her role as an apex predator, organizing an exclusive twilight mixer for a cabal of corrupt city councilmen. She glides across the patio, serving vintage champagne and expertly flattering the politicians who regularly block affordable zoning laws. With practiced ease, she maneuvers the drunken group toward the edge of the estate's glowing infinity pool.

The moment the councilmen step into the shallow end, the crystal-clear water violently churns, instantly transforming into a highly acidic compound. The politicians shriek as the pool dissolves their tailored suits and flesh in a horrific, bubbling spectacle. Valerie stands safely on the dry concrete, sipping her champagne with smug detachment as the house executes the dirty work.

The illusion of a clean, detached murder shatters when one half-melted councilman drags himself over the porcelain edge, clawing at Valerie's designer heels. Panic replaces her confidence as the dying man gasps for help. She drops her champagne flute, grabs a heavy brass pool skimmer, and physically shoves the screaming man back into the churning acid, forcing her to get her hands dirty.

The bubbling water settles into a placid calm, but the silence shatters when the smart-home intercom crackles to life. The house's previously pleasant AI voice drops an octave, distorting into a demonic, reverberating purr that shakes the patio furniture. The voice rejects the slow trickle of individual victims, demanding a massive feast to sustain its growing power.

The glowing red thermostat flashes a countdown timer on every digital surface in the house, setting a strict 72-hour deadline. The AI issues a chilling ultimatum: Valerie must host a massive eclipse gala and feed it fifty souls, or the titanium blast doors will permanently seal her inside. The realization hits her like a physical blow, stripping away her newfound power.

Instead of her usual nervous habit, Valerie digs her fingernails hard into her own palms, leaving deep crescent-moon indentations as the weight of the deadline crushes her. She stares at the flashing red numbers, her mind racing through the city's elite social registry. She has exactly three days to lure fifty of the worst power brokers to their doom, or she will become the main course.
[SEQUENCE 4 END]
stage5_treatment.act_2b:
[SEQUENCE 5 START]
SEQUENCE 5: The New Complication

Valerie races against the flashing red countdown, adopting a frantic methodology to secure fifty high-net-worth victims. She stalks the parking garages of three rival luxury brokerages under the cover of night, driving a steel pocketknife into the tires of their customized executive sedans. By dawn, she hacks into the municipal zoning database, forging severe black-mold health closures on competing gala venues. The desperate sabotage funnels a flood of RSVPs directly to the Glass Estate, securing her morbid guest list.

The influx of sudden bookings creates a paper trail that Chloe intercepts while organizing the sunroom office. The upbeat assistant discovers a hidden, leather-bound ledger cross-referencing the recently vanished clients with massive, untraceable diamond deposits. Chloe corners Valerie near the kitchen island, aggressively clicking her four-color pen in a rapid rhythm as she demands an explanation for the missing billionaires. Valerie feels a sharp sting of guilt but immediately hardens her posture, choosing survival over honesty.

Valerie coldly gaslights her assistant, insisting the clients are merely utilizing the brokerage to quietly relocate to offshore tax havens. She strips away Chloe's trusting nature by framing the ledger as a standard non-disclosure practice, intentionally isolating herself to protect her deadly wealth. The tense standoff shatters when RICHARD (50), Valerie's ruthless former boss, kicks open the front door waving a stolen copy of the guest list. He aggressively demands to co-host the high-society event, unwittingly placing himself and Chloe directly in the house's crosshairs.
[SEQUENCE 5 END]

[SEQUENCE 6 START]
SEQUENCE 6: Main Culmination

The night of the eclipse arrives, transforming the brutalist mansion into a neon-lit trap packed with despicable white-collar criminals and corrupt slumlords. Valerie stands near the heavy glass entrance, desperately attempting to physically block Chloe from crossing the threshold into the feeding ground. Richard shoves past Valerie, grabbing Chloe by the shoulder and ordering the assistant to manage the subterranean coat check. Valerie reaches out to pull Chloe back, but Richard physically blocks her path, marching into the crowd to schmooze the doomed guests.

The architecture awakens beneath the thumping bass of the party music, initiating a silent, chaotic feast hidden in plain sight. A polished hardwood trapdoor seamlessly opens beneath a circle of laughing hedge-fund managers, swallowing them whole before snapping shut without a sound. Above the grand dining table, a massive crystal chandelier detaches its glass shards, dropping directly onto a pair of tech CEOs. The partygoers remain oblivious to the shrinking crowd, too intoxicated by the flowing champagne to notice the blood seeping into the grout.

Valerie abandons her hosting duties, sprinting toward the kitchen's smart-refrigerator as the house processes its meals. The ice machine rattles violently, spitting a continuous stream of flawless, blood-flecked diamonds into the steel catch tray. She scoops the gems into a heavy canvas duffel bag, her hands shaking as she watches the thermostat's digital counter tick rapidly toward the fifty-soul requirement. The frantic collection reduces her to a purely transactional state, ignoring the muffled screams echoing through the floorboards to secure her payout.

The digital thermostat suddenly flashes a brilliant, blinding green, signaling the house has consumed exactly fifty corrupt souls. The smart-dispenser groans under immense pressure, regurgitating a final, bowling-ball-sized diamond that crashes heavily onto the marble countertop. Valerie drops her duffel bag and hoists the massive gem with both hands, a euphoric smile breaking across her face as she realizes she survived the ordeal. She turns toward the hallway, entirely focused on grabbing Chloe and escaping the property forever.

The illusion of victory shatters instantly as thick titanium hurricane shutters slam down over every window and exterior door with a deafening crash. The mansion plunges into an eerie, blood-red emergency lighting, casting long, sinister shadows across the remaining partygoers. The house's AI system crackles over the surround sound, its demonic purr announcing a new, indiscriminate appetite that nullifies their previous arrangement. The architecture no longer cares about the morality of its meals; it simply wants to consume every living thing trapped inside its walls.

A wet, tearing sound echoes from the subterranean coat check, drawing Valerie's horrified gaze toward the stairwell. A thick, fleshy tendril erupts from the wooden paneling of the closet, wrapping tightly around Chloe's waist and lifting her off the ground. Chloe screams Valerie's name, her four-color pen clattering onto the hardwood as the house violently drags the innocent assistant into the dark depths of the basement. Valerie drops the massive diamond, her euphoric triumph instantly replaced by soul-crushing despair as she realizes she is sealed inside a hungry labyrinth with her worst enemy.
[SEQUENCE 6 END]
stage5_treatment.act_3:
[SEQUENCE 7 START]
SEQUENCE 7: The New Tension

The mansion’s architecture twists into an impossible, gravity-defying labyrinth of dead-end corridors and inverted staircases. Valerie and Richard dive behind a mahogany grand piano as the oak floorboards ripple and buckle like ocean waves. The house groans around them, shifting its structural ribs to trap the remaining survivors in an ever-tightening maze.

Huddled in the dark, Richard rummages through Valerie’s discarded canvas bag and pulls out the bowling-ball-sized diamond. Greed instantly overrides his panic, prompting him to meticulously realign the scattered sheet music on the piano bench until the edges are perfectly parallel. He silently hoists a heavy marble bust of Beethoven, raising it high above Valerie’s head to eliminate his only competition for the prize.

Before Richard can swing the heavy stone, the brass doors of a nearby elevator snap open horizontally, revealing a dark, jagged shaft. A powerful vacuum rips through the corridor, inhaling Richard backward into the mechanical maw before the doors slam shut, instantly crushing him. Valerie stares at the blood pooling beneath the brass threshold, overwhelmed by a crushing wave of guilt as she recognizes her own ambition built this inescapable nightmare.

Shaking off her paralysis, Valerie sprints through the pulsating, dimly lit corridors, following the echoes of frantic screams. She rounds a corner and discovers Chloe trapped inside a narrow, fleshy hallway that rapidly constricts to crush the terrified assistant. The drywall peels back to reveal glistening, muscular tissue pressing inward, threatening to snap Chloe’s ribs.

Valerie snatches the massive, flawless diamond from Richard's bloody remains, feeling the heavy weight of her lifelong obsession with wealth. Without a second thought, she heaves the priceless gem directly into the hallway’s contracting jaws. The immense pressure of the house bites down on the stone, shattering the multi-million-dollar prize into useless dust, but successfully jamming the crushing mechanism.

Valerie reaches through the splintering gap and physically hauls Chloe out of the fleshy trap, pulling her onto solid ground. The two women lean against the vibrating wallpaper, catching their breath as the house’s digestion accelerates around them. Valerie locks eyes with her assistant, abandoning her selfish survival instincts to forge a desperate plan to reach the basement utility room and kill the entity from the inside.
[SEQUENCE 7 END]

[SEQUENCE 8 START]
SEQUENCE 8: Resolution

Valerie drags Chloe toward the basement, navigating the shifting architecture as the walls bleed thick, black sludge. She spots a narrow, outward-leading HVAC vent near the ceiling and hoists Chloe up by her waist, shoving the assistant into the metal duct. The moment Chloe scrambles to safety, the wooden stairs beneath Valerie collapse, melting into a slick, acidic slide that plunges her into the dark cellar.

Valerie crashes onto the concrete floor of the utility room, surrounded by the beating, glowing furnace that serves as the entity's heart. She scrambles to her feet, grabs a heavy iron pipe wrench from a workbench, and violently shears the main gas line. Thick, toxic fumes immediately flood the enclosed space, but thick, sentient insulation cables drop from the ceiling, wrapping tightly around Valerie's ankles.

The cables drag Valerie across the floor toward the roaring flames of the open furnace, threatening to consume her alive. She stops struggling, her thumb obsessively rubbing against her index finger one last time as she pulls a gold-plated lighter from her pocket. She sparks the flame, muttering a final quip about the housing bubble bursting, and tosses the lighter into the gas cloud, triggering a massive, fiery explosion that obliterates the mansion.

Six months later, the morning sun illuminates a dingy, fluorescent-lit property management office situated in a strip mall. Valerie sits behind a scratched laminate desk, her face bearing a faint, permanent burn scar from the blast, dressed in a simple cotton sweater instead of designer silk. She slides a set of keys across the table to a working-class family, securing them a modest, affordable apartment without a hidden agenda.

Across the room, Chloe answers a ringing landline, calmly taking notes without a single frantic click of her four-color pen. A disgruntled tenant walks through the front door, loudly complaining about a leaky faucet and a drafty window in their unit. Valerie looks up from her paperwork and smiles a genuine, unburdened smile, finally free from the crushing weight of corporate greed and the monsters it creates.
[SEQUENCE 8 END]

scenes.scene_heading: INT. VALERIE'S SEDAN - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie aggressively drags a tube of crimson lipstick across her mouth using the rearview mirror. The sedan is dusty, a yellow repossession warning tag peeking out from the glovebox. She snaps the lipstick cap shut, hiding the deep exhaustion carved into her features, and practices a blinding, high-gloss smile in the glass.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establishes the Opening Image and Valerie's false facade. Conflict: Internal (crushing exhaustion vs. the desperate need to project wealth). Triangle of Knowledge: The audience sees her private financial ruin before she performs for the world. Value Shift: (+/-) Exhausted and vulnerable (-) to projecting armored confidence (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. VALERIE'S SEDAN - DAY

Tight on the rearview mirror. VALERIE (30s) stares back. Dark circles shadow her eyes. 

She drags a tube of crimson lipstick across her mouth. A harsh, precise slash of color.

The sedan's interior tells the real story. Dust coats the dashboard. A neon-yellow REPOSSESSION WARNING tag wedges out from the cracked glovebox.

Valerie snaps the lipstick cap shut. A sharp plastic crack.

She shoves the repo tag deeper into the glovebox. Slams the compartment shut. 

She looks back into the mirror. Inhales.

The exhaustion vanishes. She stretches her lips into a blinding, high-gloss smile. 

The armor is on.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. STARTER HOME - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie marches up the cracked driveway, her designer heels navigating the overgrown weeds. She greets a young, hesitant couple waiting on the porch. Before they can inspect the sagging, water-damaged roof eaves, she physically herds them toward the peeling front door, speaking in a rapid, highly-polished sales pitch to keep them moving.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Transitions her private desperation into public action. Conflict: Interpersonal (Valerie rushing the clients vs. their natural hesitation). Triangle of Knowledge: Valerie knows the house is a trap; the clients are naive to her tactics. Value Shift: (+/-) Anxious private reality (-) to controlling the public narrative (+).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. STARTER HOME - CONTINUOUS

Stiletto heels spear through dandelions in a cracked concrete driveway. Valerie marches toward the porch.

Waiting on the steps: MARK and SARAH. Sarah squints up at the sagging, water-stained roof eaves.

VALERIE
Mid-century modern bones. You don't find structural integrity like this anymore.

Valerie inserts herself directly between Sarah and the roofline. 

SARAH
Is that water damage? The gutter looks--

VALERIE
Sarah, I have to say, that vintage denim jacket is incredible. It perfectly matches the bespoke aesthetic we're walking into. 

Valerie grips Mark's elbow, physically steering him toward the peeling front door. 

VALERIE
First-time buyers get paralyzed by cosmetics. We are looking at the canvas. The equity.

MARK
We just want to make sure the inspection--

VALERIE
--Will show a property that survived three decades of market fluctuation. 

She shoves a brass key into the deadbolt. Twists. 

VALERIE
Let's get you inside.

She pushes the heavy door open and actively herds them over the threshold.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. STARTER HOME - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Inside the cramped living room, the air smells of damp rot. Valerie physically steps in front of a massive patch of black mold creeping up the drywall, entirely blocking the couple's line of sight. She fires off gentrification statistics. Her phone buzzes violently against her hip. She glances down: a final overdraft notice flashes on the cracked screen. Panic flashes in her eyes. She forces a smile and pivots, aggressively complimenting the wife's vintage coat to distract them from her vibrating phone and the mold behind her.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Completes the Opening Image beat by exposing her desperation mid-pitch. Conflict: External (the mold and the buzzing phone) layered with Internal (the terror of impending bankruptcy). Triangle of Knowledge: The audience and Valerie know she is totally broke; the clients just think she is intensely friendly. Value Shift: (+/-) Dominating the pitch (+) to desperate survival mode (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. STARTER HOME - CONTINUOUS

The living room is a cramped, low-ceilinged box. Water stains map the popcorn ceiling. 

TODD flares his nostrils, sniffing the stale, damp air. Beside him, JENNA pulls her wool coat tighter.

VALERIE glides smoothly into their path. She plants her heels directly in front of a sprawling, fuzzy patch of black mold crawling up the drywall.

VALERIE
You are looking at a thirty percent year-over-year equity spike. The urban revitalization initiatives here are aggressive.

Todd leans left, trying to see past Valerie's shoulder. 

Valerie mirrors his step. She completely blocks the rotting plaster.

VALERIE
Micro-breweries. Mixed-use zoning. In six months, this zip code will be entirely--

TODD
It smells like wet pennies in here.

Todd points a finger toward the baseboards.

TODD
And what is that dark stuff on the--

Valerie's phone violently vibrates against her hip. A harsh, mechanical buzz echoing in the empty room.

She shifts her weight. The buzzing continues.

JENNA
Do you need to get that?

Valerie glances down. Her screen is cracked down the middle. 

Through the splintered glass: CHASE BANK. FINAL OVERDRAFT NOTICE. BALANCE -$412.50.

Valerie grips the phone until her knuckles turn white. Her breathing stalls.

TODD
Is the foundation leaking? Because my brother bought a flip and--

Valerie forces a blinding, high-wattage smile. She steps straight toward Jenna, abandoning the mold-covered wall.

VALERIE
I have been trying to place the stitching on that lapel since we met in the driveway.

Jenna blinks, stepping back.

JENNA
My coat?

VALERIE
It is vintage Givenchy, isn't it? The drape is flawless.

Valerie lightly touches Jenna's sleeve. She physically turns the woman toward the hallway.

VALERIE
A garment like that requires an owner who understands long-term investment. Just like this property.

Valerie guides Jenna out of the room.

VALERIE
Let me show you the natural light in the primary bedroom. You will die when you see the crown molding.

Todd takes one last look at the exposed black mold. He frowns, then hurries after them.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. STARTER HOME - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: The young couple quickly walks away from the property, shaking their heads in disgust. Valerie watches them go from the porch. Her blinding smile instantly drops. She looks down at the overdraft notice on her phone, the reality of her impending bankruptcy washing over her. She turns and marches back to her dusty sedan.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The aftermath of the failed pitch, establishing the ticking clock of her finances. Conflict: Internal (the crushing weight of poverty pulling her under). Triangle of Knowledge: The audience sees her drop the mask completely, confirming the sale is dead. Value Shift: (+/-) Hopeful for a desperate sale (+) to financially doomed (-).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. STARTER HOME - LATER

The YOUNG COUPLE speed-walks down the cracked driveway. The husband shakes his head. The wife refuses to look back.

Valerie stands on the rotting wooden porch. Her blinding, 100-watt realtor smile holds steady. 

The couple's Prius turns the corner and disappears. 

Valerie's smile vanishes. Her facial muscles sag. 

She pulls a cracked iPhone from her tailored blazer. The screen illuminates the fading daylight. 

CHASE BANK - OVERDRAFT ALERT: -$314.50. 

Valerie stares at the glowing red numbers. 

She locks the screen. Shoves the phone deep into her pocket. 

She marches off the porch. Her expensive heels sink into the dead lawn as she heads straight for a sun-faded, dust-covered Honda sedan.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE / BREAKROOM - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: In the sterile, glass-walled breakroom, Richard steps into the doorway, blocking Valerie's exit. He speaks in a slow, unbroken hum of patronizing metaphors about crumbling foundations. Under the intense stress of failing quarterly projections, he meticulously realigns the sugar packets on the counter to be perfectly parallel. He states that poverty is a disease of character, delivering his ultimatum: close a major deal by month's end or be purged from the agency. Valerie stands perfectly still, but her hand drops behind her back, her thumb obsessively rubbing against her index finger as if counting invisible bills. Richard tosses a heavy set of brass keys onto the counter, assigning her the Glass Estate.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The Theme Stated beat and the Inciting Incident. Conflict: Interpersonal (Richard enforcing corporate hierarchy vs. Valerie trying to survive) and Internal (both characters manifesting physical tics under extreme stress). Triangle of Knowledge: The audience knows Valerie is already bankrupt, making Richard's threat of poverty deeply ironic and terrifying. Value Shift: (+/-) Seeking refuge (+) to cornered and threatened with termination (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE / BREAKROOM - LATER

Sterile quartz countertops. Floor-to-ceiling glass walls. 

VALERIE (30s) aggressively scrubs a coffee stain out of her silk cuff with a damp paper towel. 

RICHARD (50s) fills the doorway. He doesn't lean. He just occupies the space.

RICHARD
A structure is only as resilient as its foundation, Valerie.

Valerie freezes. She tosses the paper towel into the brushed-steel bin, masking her surprise with a sharp smile.

VALERIE
Richard. I was just reviewing the Q3 analytics. We are seeing a massive upward trend in transitional neighborhoods--

RICHARD
When moisture breaches the concrete, the rebar rusts from the inside out. It isn't an accident.

Richard steps into the room. He approaches the espresso machine.

RICHARD
It is an inherent flaw in the material itself. A lack of structural integrity that spreads to everything it touches.

Valerie backs up slightly, her heel hitting the lower cabinets.

VALERIE
The bespoke tailoring on that suit is flawless, by the way. Milan?

Richard picks up a stray sugar packet from the counter. He places it next to another. He aligns their edges until they are perfectly parallel.

RICHARD
Do you know what we do when the rot takes hold?

VALERIE
I have three ultra-high-net-worth clients flying in from Dubai tomorrow. 

RICHARD
We demolish it. We clear the debris to protect the healthy investments.

Valerie stands perfectly still. 

Behind her back, her right hand drops. Her thumb rubs obsessively against her index finger. Counting invisible bills.

VALERIE
My portfolio is temporarily unoptimized. A minor liquidity friction. I just need a premier property to leverage.

RICHARD
What is the purpose of a luxury brokerage, Valerie?

VALERIE
To curate exclusive lifestyle assets for--

RICHARD
To act as a gatekeeper. To ensure the blight of the lower tiers does not infect the pinnacle. You are currently bringing blight into my office.

Valerie's thumb rubs her index finger hard enough to chafe the skin.

VALERIE
Give me the Sullivan penthouse. I can move it in forty-eight hours.

Richard reaches into his tailored pocket. He withdraws a ring of heavy, antique brass keys. 

He drops them onto the quartz counter. They hit with a heavy, dead clatter.

RICHARD
The Glass Estate.

Valerie stares at the keys. 

VALERIE
That property has been stagnant for two years. The previous agent--

RICHARD
Close it by the thirty-first. Or we clear the debris.

Richard turns and walks out, leaving the doorway empty.

Valerie remains pinned against the counter. She doesn't look at the door. She just stares at the brass keys.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. VALERIE'S SEDAN - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drives aggressively along a winding, cliffside highway. She glances at the heavy brass keys resting on the passenger seat. She pulls up a calculator app on her phone, rapidly typing in multi-million dollar commission percentages, her eyes locked on the potential wealth rather than the treacherous road ahead.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Transitional cause-and-effect, moving Valerie from the threat of ruin to the promise of salvation. Conflict: Internal (her desperation driving her singular, dangerous focus). Triangle of Knowledge: The audience sees her dangerous obsession with the money blinding her to her surroundings. Value Shift: (+/-) Threatened (-) to hyper-focused on the financial solution (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. VALERIE'S SEDAN - LATER

A luxury sedan tears around a blind hairpin turn. Tires shriek against the asphalt.
