scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / INVERTED STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie and Richard push through a set of double doors, attempting to find the rear servant stairs. Instead, the staircase twists upward in an impossible, gravity-defying spiral. A heavy wooden chandelier swings upward, crashing into the ceiling. Richard shoves Valerie back into the main hallway as the steps flatten into a sheer, impassable slide, blocking their descent.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Cut off the easiest route of escape, trapping them in the maze. Layered Micro-Conflict: (><) Richard tries to dictate the escape route vs. the architecture actively defying physics vs. Valerie's growing distrust of his leadership. Triangle of Knowledge: The house is revealing its impossible geometry, proving their standard floor plans are useless. Value Shift: (+/-) From hopeful escape to trapped desperation.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. GLASS ESTATE / INVERTED STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS

Valerie and Richard shove through heavy oak double doors. 

Richard holds a crumpled blueprint. He smooths the creases flat against his palm.

RICHARD
The servant quarters anchor the east wing. A proper foundation always provides a secondary egress.

Valerie taps her dead cell phone against her thigh. 

VALERIE
Secondary egress implies a functional floor plan--

She stops dead. 

The staircase does not descend. The mahogany steps twist upward in a sickening, gravity-defying corkscrew. 

Richard steps to the edge. He traces the polished banister. 

RICHARD
Fascinating structural choices. The framing is entirely inverted. 

A heavy wooden chandelier hangs above them. 

The iron chain groans. The massive fixture swings upward. 

It smashes into the ceiling. Glass and carved wood rain down. 

Valerie flinches. She shields her face with her clipboard. 

VALERIE
Impeccable craftsmanship. Really holds up under pressure. 

The staircase shudders. The mahogany planks shift and merge. 

The ninety-degree angles of the stairs flatten out. The entire flight transforms into a sheer, impassable slide. 

Richard grabs Valerie by the shoulder. He shoves her backward. 

They stumble back through the double doors into the main hallway. 

The doors slam shut. The brass locks click into place on their own. 

Richard straightens his suit jacket. He does not blink. 

RICHARD
It appears the architect prefers we remain on the primary level.

Valerie stares at the locked doors. Her breathing goes shallow. 

VALERIE
That is a stunning pocket square. Tom Ford?

Richard slowly folds the useless blueprint. 

RICHARD
We must respect the boundaries of the property.

VALERIE
The property is trying to liquidate us.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / LABYRINTHINE CORRIDORS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: They sprint down a hallway that stretches and narrows. Drywall cracks, revealing dark, shifting structural ribs behind the paint. Dead ends materialize where doors used to be. A heavy gust of wind howls down the corridor, herding them toward the only open doorway left—the music room. They dive inside just as the hallway behind them seals shut with a violent slam of timber.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Funnel the characters into an isolated, inescapable arena for the confrontation. Layered Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie and Richard seek cover vs. the house actively herding them like cattle. Triangle of Knowledge: The characters think they found a safe room; the audience suspects the house put them there on purpose. Value Shift: (+/-) From frantic running to claustrophobic entrapment.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. GLASS ESTATE / LABYRINTHINE CORRIDORS - CONTINUOUS

Valerie and Richard sprint. Hardwood floors buckle beneath their designer shoes. 

The hallway stretches. The perspective distorts, pulling the far wall further away. 

VALERIE
This wasn't on the blueprints! The east wing is supposed to terminate at the solarium--

RICHARD
(panting)
A house is only as stable as its foundation, Valerie. This one is actively rewriting the soil.

Valerie grabs the brass handle of a guest room door. She twists. 

The handle sinks into the wood. The grooves smooth over. 

The entire door melts into seamless, flat drywall.

VALERIE
Where is the egress? We just need a primary exit--

A jagged crack rips down the ceiling. 

Plaster rains down on Richard’s tailored suit. Behind the split drywall, thick, black structural ribs pulse. 

They look like iron, but they flex like muscle.

RICHARD
Fascinating. The framing is entirely organic.

VALERIE
Are you evaluating the structural integrity right now? Your tie is covered in plaster, move!

The walls press inward. The corridor narrows. Three feet wide. Two feet. 

Valerie pushes ahead. Richard follows, his chest brushing both sides of the shrinking passage. 

A howling draft tears through the corridor. It hits them from behind, a gale smelling of copper and dust. 

The gust shoves Valerie forward. She stumbles, her heels skidding on the polished oak. 

At the end of the shrinking tunnel, a single set of double doors stands wide open. The Music Room.

RICHARD
It is funneling us. An architectural bottleneck designed to dictate our movement.

VALERIE
I don't care about the traffic flow, Richard! Get inside!

The walls groan, closing in to crush them. 

Valerie dives through the double doors. Richard lunges right behind her. 

They hit the carpeted floor. 

Behind them, thick timber slams across the doorway. Iron nails shoot through the wood, sealing the frame shut with a deafening crack.

Silence returns, thick and heavy. 

Valerie scrambles to her feet. She aggressively adjusts the lapels of her blazer.

VALERIE
Okay. High vaulted ceilings. Premium acoustics. We can work with this layout.

Richard slowly stands. He brushes the drywall dust from his sleeves. 

RICHARD
We are exactly where it wants us to be.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / MUSIC ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie and Richard huddle in the dark behind a mahogany grand piano. The floorboards buckle. Richard rummages through Valerie's discarded canvas bag and pulls out the massive diamond. He stares at it, his breathing slowing. He meticulously realigns the scattered sheet music on the piano bench until the edges are perfectly parallel. Without a word, he hoists a heavy marble bust of Beethoven, raising it high above Valerie's head. Before he can swing, the brass elevator doors across the room snap open horizontally. A powerful vacuum rips through the room, inhaling Richard backward. He screams as the doors slam shut, instantly crushing him. Blood pools beneath the brass threshold. The diamond rolls across the floor.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Execute the Dark Night of the Soul climax; eliminate the main human antagonist. Layered Micro-Conflict: (><) Richard attempts to murder Valerie for the prize vs. Valerie's unawareness vs. the house consuming the greediest player. Triangle of Knowledge: The audience sees Richard raise the bust before Valerie does, creating suspense before the house intervenes. Value Shift: (+/-) From tense hiding to brutal, bloody shock.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. GLASS ESTATE / MUSIC ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Pitch black. The groan of stressed timber echoes through the dark.

VALERIE and RICHARD crouch behind a massive mahogany grand piano. 

The floorboards beneath them ripple. They roll like waves trapped under a carpet.

Valerie grips the piano leg. Her knuckles turn white.

VALERIE
The floor plan is actively hostile. We need to pivot to the service corridor--

Richard ignores her. He drags Valerie's discarded canvas bag across the buckling parquet floor. 

He plunges his hands inside the canvas.

VALERIE
Leave the staging materials. We have a zero-percent survival rate if we remain stationary.

RICHARD
A house is only as enduring as its foundation, Valerie. When the bedrock shifts, the architect must salvage the premium assets.

Richard pulls his hand from the bag. 

The massive diamond rests in his palm. It catches a sliver of moonlight.

Richard's breathing slows. The chaos of the groaning walls fades from his attention.

VALERIE
Put that down. It is a massive liability. The seller is highly motivated to kill us.

RICHARD
Is it not the fundamental nature of this city to extract beauty from the collapse? 

Valerie peers around the piano leg. She tracks a violent tremor moving through the plaster ceiling.

Richard places the diamond on the piano bench. Next to it lies a scattered pile of Beethoven sheet music.

His hands tremble slightly. He stares at the messy pile of paper.

With meticulous precision, Richard slides the pages together. He taps the edges against the wood until they are perfectly parallel.

Valerie keeps her back to him, scanning the shadows.

VALERIE
The market is crashing, Richard. We are depreciating assets if we stay in this room.

Richard's eyes lock onto a heavy marble bust of Beethoven resting on the piano lid.

He wraps his fingers around the cold stone base. He lifts it in silence.

The bust rises high above Valerie's head.

RICHARD
We are simply restructuring the hierarchy.

He shifts his weight to swing.

Across the room, brass elevator doors SNAP OPEN horizontally.

A gale-force vacuum erupts from the black shaft. 

The suction rips through the room. The perfectly aligned sheet music explodes into the air.

Richard is yanked backward off his feet. 

The marble bust slips from his grip. It shatters against the floorboards.

He flies backward through the dark. He screams.

The brass doors SLAM SHUT.

A wet, sickening crunch echoes.

Silence returns, save for the settling dust.

From beneath the polished brass threshold, a thick pool of crimson blood seeps onto the hardwood.

The massive diamond rolls across the slanted floor.

It clinks against Valerie's shoe.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / MUSIC ROOM - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie sits perfectly still in the dark, staring at the blood seeping under the elevator doors. She looks at the flawless diamond resting on the buckling floorboards. She doesn't reach for it with her usual frantic greed. Instead, she slowly picks it up, feeling the heavy, sickening weight of it. She recognizes that her own relentless ambition built this inescapable nightmare.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Provide a quiet aftermath for the protagonist to experience profound moral clarity. Layered Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie's lifelong obsession with wealth vs. the undeniable, bloody cost of that wealth lying right in front of her. Triangle of Knowledge: Valerie finally understands the thematic truth the audience has known: her greed is the true monster. Value Shift: (+/-) From blind ambition to crushing guilt and clarity.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. GLASS ESTATE / MUSIC ROOM - LATER

Pitch black. The groan of settling steel. 

A thick, dark liquid pools out from beneath the brushed-steel elevator doors. 

Valerie sits on a velvet piano bench. Her posture is rigid. The designer suit she wears is ruined, splattered with crimson.

She watches the puddle inch across the imported hardwood.

A few feet away, resting on a warped, buckling floorboard, sits a diamond. The size of a quail egg. Flawless.

Her phone vibrates in her pocket. The screen casts a harsh, blue light across her face. Caller ID: CHLOE.

Valerie answers. Puts it on speaker. Sets the phone on the piano keys.

CHLOE
Valerie, I pulled the escrow files, the offshore transfers cleared but the zoning board is threatening a penalty fee if we don't submit the structural addendum by midnight--

Valerie stares at the diamond. She doesn't speak.

CHLOE
--and Richard's office called twice, he wants the Q3 projections, I told them you were at the estate but they're pushing for an in-person meeting tomorrow at eight--

The blood reaches the toe of Valerie's pump.

VALERIE
Chloe.

CHLOE
--I can stall them, I have the binders ready, I just need your signature on the liability waivers--

VALERIE
Stop.

Silence on the line. 

Just the hum of the house. A low, rhythmic thrumming deep within the walls. Digestion.

CHLOE
Are you okay? You sound... did the showing go well?

Valerie looks at her own hands. Her thumb twitches toward her index finger. The old habit. Counting invisible bills.

She feels the sticky, wet blood coating her skin. The friction stops the movement.

VALERIE
Cancel the morning appointments.

CHLOE
All of them? Richard will--

VALERIE
Cancel them.

Valerie ends the call. The blue light dies.

She slides off the bench. Her knees hit the floorboards. The blood soaks through her stockings.

She doesn't lunge for the gemstone. She reaches out with a slow, trembling hand.

Her fingers close around the diamond.

It catches the faint moonlight. Perfect. Cold.

Valerie closes her fist around it. The sharp cut of the stone bites into her palm. She doesn't let go. 

She lowers her head, alone in the dark with the heavy, sickening weight of it.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / PULSATING CORRIDORS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie shakes off her paralysis. She shoves the heavy gem into her bag and sprints back out into the dimly lit hallways. The walls throb with a slow, rhythmic pulse. She stops at an intersection, listening intently, and locks onto the faint, echoing sound of frantic screams. She breaks into a full sprint toward the noise.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Transition from passive survivor to active rescuer; initiate the Break Into Three. Layered Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie's physical exhaustion vs. her desperate need to save Chloe vs. the disorienting acoustics of the house. Triangle of Knowledge: Valerie knows Chloe is alive, giving her a singular, selfless mission. Value Shift: (+/-) From isolated despair to focused, selfless momentum.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. GLASS ESTATE / PULSATING CORRIDORS - CONTINUOUS

Valerie stands frozen in the dim light. Her chest heaves. A fist-sized, flawless diamond rests in her trembling palm.

She blinks. The shock shatters.

She shoves the heavy gem deep into her leather tote bag and bolts. 

The architecture twists around her. The corridor stretches too far. The sleek, modernist drywall expands and contracts. A slow, rhythmic pulse.

Valerie's heels skid on the polished hardwood. She hits a four-way intersection and slams her hands against the doorframes to brake.

She stops. Her lungs burn. She clamps a hand over her own mouth to muffle her ragged breathing. 

She leans against the throbbing wall. Listening.

The house groans. Wood settling. Glass straining.

Then-- a scream. Faint, frantic, bouncing off the vaulted ceilings. 

Chloe.

Valerie's head snaps toward the west corridor. The exhaustion drains from her posture.

She drops the leather tote bag. It hits the floor with a heavy thud. The diamond spills out, catching the dim overhead light.

Valerie doesn't look down. She abandons the gem and breaks into a dead, reckless sprint toward the screams.

SMASH CUT TO:

scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / FLESHY HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie rounds a corner and finds Chloe trapped inside a narrow corridor. The drywall peels back in wet strips, revealing glistening, muscular tissue pressing inward. The walls rapidly constrict, threatening to snap Chloe's ribs. Valerie pulls the bowling-ball-sized diamond from her bag. She heaves the priceless gem directly into the hallway's contracting jaws. The immense pressure of the house bites down, shattering the multi-million-dollar stone into useless dust, but successfully jamming the crushing mechanism. Valerie reaches through the splintering gap and physically hauls Chloe out.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The physical manifestation of Valerie rejecting her sin (wealth) to save an innocent. Layered Micro-Conflict: (><) The house attempts to digest Chloe vs. Valerie sacrificing her ultimate prize to break the trap. Triangle of Knowledge: Valerie proves to Chloe (and the audience) that she has fundamentally changed by destroying the diamond. Value Shift: (+/-) From impending death to hard-won survival.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. GLASS ESTATE / FLESHY HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS

Valerie rounds the corner, heels skidding on slick hardwood. She freezes.

The hallway is breathing. 

Plaster and drywall peel back in long, wet strips. Beneath the pristine white paint, thick cords of red, glistening muscle tissue press inward.

Chloe is pinned midway down the corridor. 

The walls contract. Chloe's arms are trapped against her sides. Clear, acidic mucus soaks her tailored blazer.

CHLOE
Valerie-- the lateral pressure is compressing at roughly four inches per second and human ribs fracture at three hundred and thirty pounds of--

VALERIE
Stop the math. Give me your hand.

Valerie lunges forward. She grabs Chloe's wrist. 

The walls pulse. A wet, tearing sound echoes. The corridor violently narrows another inch. 

Chloe screams. Her face goes pale.

CHLOE
The structural integrity is gone-- it's digesting the load-bearing beams and--

VALERIE
Look at me! 

Valerie braces her boots against the baseboard. She pulls. 

The fleshy walls grip Chloe tighter. A sickening crack echoes. Chloe gasps, spitting a speck of blood.

Valerie loses her footing. She falls backward. Her designer tote bag hits the floor. 

The bowling-ball-sized diamond spills out. It hits the floorboards with a heavy, dull thud. 

It catches the overhead recessed lighting. Flawless. Priceless.

Valerie stares at the stone. Her ticket out. Her shield against the world.

The hallway groans. The muscle fibers ripple, preparing for a final, crushing spasm. 

CHLOE
(wheezing)
I can't--

Valerie grabs the massive diamond. She doesn't hesitate. 

She steps into the narrowing gap. She heaves the multi-million-dollar stone directly into the contracting jaws of the hallway, wedging it inches from Chloe's torso.

The house bites down.

The walls slam into the diamond. The floorboards buckle. 

For a second, the stone holds. 

Then-- a deafening crack. 

The flawless diamond shatters. It explodes into millions of useless, glittering shards. 

Diamond dust rains down, coating Chloe's hair and the wet, pulsing walls. 

The crushing mechanism jams. The walls twitch, stuck on the jagged debris. 

Valerie reaches through the splintering gap. She grabs Chloe by the collar of her ruined blazer. 

With one brutal heave, Valerie drags her out. 

They collapse onto the safe, solid hardwood of the landing. 

Chloe coughs violently. She clutches her chest. 

CHLOE
You destroyed the primary asset. 

Valerie wipes a smear of diamond dust and blood from her own cheek. She stares at the ruined hallway.

VALERIE
The buyer wanted an open-concept floor plan anyway. 

Chloe lets out a ragged, breathless sob. 

CHLOE
My itinerary is completely ruined and I haven't updated the master calendar--

Valerie pulls Chloe up by the arm. 

VALERIE
We're canceling the open house.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / VIBRATING ALCOVE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie and Chloe collapse against a section of vibrating wallpaper, sliding down to the floor. They cough on the drywall dust, catching their breath as the house's digestion accelerates, the walls groaning around them. Valerie locks eyes with her assistant. She abandons her polished sales-pitch persona entirely. They quickly map out the remaining architecture and forge a desperate plan: reach the basement utility room and ignite the gas lines to kill the entity from the inside.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establish the Dangling Cause and the final objective for Act III. Layered Micro-Conflict: (><) The overwhelming physical deterioration of the house vs. Valerie and Chloe's newfound unified resolve. Triangle of Knowledge: The characters now share a definitive, suicidal plan to destroy the house, setting the stakes for the finale. Value Shift: (+/-) From terrified victims to determined saboteurs.
scenes.draft_text:
INT. GLASS ESTATE / VIBRATING ALCOVE - CONTINUOUS

The silk wallpaper ripples like a breathing lung. The floorboards heave.

Valerie and Chloe press their backs against the shrinking space. 

CHLOE
The structural load-bearing walls are shifting at three-second intervals and if the square footage decreases by another ten percent--

VALERIE
Fluid floor plan. We just have to force the egress.

Valerie lunges. She snatches a heavy brass candlestick from a mahogany side table. 

She jams the thick base into the shrinking seam between the wall panels.

CHLOE
If we miscalculate the compression ratio by even a fraction of a second the drywall is going to--

VALERIE
I love that blazer on you, Chloe. 

Chloe blinks, thrown. 

VALERIE
Very sharp. Now grab the brass.

Chloe grips the candlestick next to Valerie. 

A deep, wet groan rumbles through the foundation. The walls contract.

VALERIE
Push!

They throw their combined weight against the makeshift lever. 

The brass bends. The house shrieks in grinding timber.

Plaster tears. A narrow gap splits open.

Valerie shoves Chloe through the breach. 

Valerie dives after her. 

They hit the hardwood of the main corridor. 

Behind them, the alcove snaps shut with the crack of breaking teeth. 

Valerie scrambles to her feet. 

VALERIE
Showings aren't over. Keep moving!

They sprint down the hallway.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / VIBRATING ALCOVE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie and Chloe push off the vibrating wallpaper. Valerie grabs a heavy brass candlestick from a side table for leverage. They coordinate their timing with the house's rhythmic groans, bursting out of the alcove and sprinting into the main corridor.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Transition from the planning phase of the previous climax into the execution of the final run. Micro-Conflict: (><) External: The house's shifting architecture vs. Their need to move. Internal: Valerie overcoming her fear of the entity. Value Shift: (+/-) From trapped (-) to proactive (+).
scenes.draft_text:

Showings aren't over. Keep moving!

scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The architecture shifts aggressively. Black sludge bleeds from the crown molding. Valerie and Chloe sprint, dodging buckling floorboards. Valerie pulls Chloe backward by the collar just as a heavy wooden doorway snaps shut like a jaw, splintering the frame.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Escalate the ticking clock of the house's digestion. Micro-Conflict: (><) External: The hostile, shifting walls vs. their forward momentum. Triangle of Knowledge: The audience sees the door frame warp and prepare to snap shut seconds before Valerie notices and pulls Chloe back. Value Shift: (+/-) From proactive (+) to endangered (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. GLASS ESTATE / UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS

The corridor actively narrows. Crown molding cracks overhead. Thick black sludge oozes from the plaster seams, dripping onto the imported runner rug.

VALERIE and CHLOE sprint through the dripping mess.

CHLOE
The structural blueprints didn't account for this much lateral movement, the entire second-floor joist system is--

VALERIE
Push through it! It's just a transitional space!

Oak floorboards buckle under their heels. Hardwood planks snap upward. 

Ahead, a heavy mahogany doorway waits at the end of the hall.

The trim bends inward. The hinges stretch. The wood groans, bowing under immense unseen pressure.

Chloe doesn't see it. She pumps her arms, sprinting straight for the opening.

CHLOE
If we hit the primary suite we can leverage the fire escape and bypass--

Valerie spots the warping frame. She lunges.

Her hand catches the collar of Chloe's blazer. She yanks her backward.

The mahogany door slams shut. It doesn't swing--it snaps sideways like a jaw.

Wood explodes. Splinters shower the hallway. 

Chloe hits the floor hard. Valerie drags her back another two feet.

Dust settles. The doorframe is crushed into jagged wooden teeth. Black sludge drips from the ruined hinges.

Valerie wipes plaster dust from her tailored lapel.

VALERIE
The primary suite is officially off the market.

Chloe stares at the splintered wood. Her hands tremble as she pushes herself up from the floorboards.

CHLOE
The structural load just doubled. We need a secondary egress. Now.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / GRAND FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: They reach the ground floor. The marble tiles crack open, revealing fleshy, acidic gaps beneath the foundation. Valerie uses the candlestick to test the floor's stability, firmly guiding Chloe across the treacherous expanse toward the servant's corridor.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Demonstrate Valerie's leadership and physical protection of Chloe, cementing her moral shift from selfish to selfless. Micro-Conflict: (><) External: The cracking floor vs. safe passage. Interpersonal: Chloe hesitates at the edge of a gap, and Valerie must command her to jump. Value Shift: (+/-) From endangered (-) to unified (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. GLASS ESTATE / GRAND FOYER - CONTINUOUS

Valerie and Chloe hit the bottom of the grand staircase. 

The Calacatta marble floor shudders. A web of deep fissures shoots across the pristine white stone.

Tiles snap and heave upward. 

Beneath the shattered luxury: a pulsing, wet trench of crimson tissue. Yellow steam hisses from the gap, eating the edges of the marble.

Valerie grips a heavy brass candlestick. She steps forward, slamming the brass base onto an intact tile. 

Solid. 

VALERIE
Minor settling. The foundation is highly motivated. 

Valerie grabs Chloe's wrist, pulling her onto the tested tile. 

CHLOE
The tensile strength of unreinforced stone drops by eighty percent when subjected to corrosive environments and I weigh exactly one hundred and thirty-two pounds--

VALERIE
We are pivoting to the servant's corridor. Unbeatable high-traffic durability. Step here.

Valerie swings the candlestick again. 

Crack. A tile crumbles into the acidic trench. 

She adjusts her aim, strikes the adjacent slab. It holds. She yanks Chloe forward. 

They navigate the labyrinth of shifting stone. The floor groans. The fleshy gaps widen, bubbling with digestive heat.

A massive trench splits open, directly blocking the arched doorway to the servant's quarters. Five feet across. 

Valerie leaps first. Her heels skid on the far edge, but she balances. She turns back.

Chloe stands stranded on the shrinking island of marble. The edges dissolve into the steaming red maw. 

VALERIE
Come on. 

CHLOE
The statistical probability of clearing a gap this wide without proper footwear or a running start--

VALERIE
I love those loafers on you. The stitching is impeccable. 

Chloe stares down into the bubbling acid. Her breathing goes shallow. 

VALERIE
Look at me. 

Chloe looks up. 

VALERIE
You negotiated a twelve percent reduction on the HVAC contract. You do not back down from bad terms. Close the deal. Jump.

The marble beneath Chloe tilts. 

Chloe lunges. 

She clears the gap, but her foot slips on the slick edge. 

Valerie snatches her by the collar, hauling her onto the solid hardwood of the corridor. 

They collapse against the wall. The foyer behind them churns, swallowing the last of the marble. 

Valerie forces herself up. She points the candlestick down the dark hallway.

VALERIE
Excellent execution. Let's tour the back half.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / SERVANT'S CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: They reach the basement door. It is swollen shut in its frame, bleeding thick sludge from the brass hinges. Valerie and Chloe throw their combined weight against the wood, repeatedly kicking the handle until the frame splinters and the door gives way.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Breaching the final threshold before entering the belly of the beast. Micro-Conflict: (><) External: The swollen door vs. their physical strength. Value Shift: (+/-) From blocked (-) to breaking through (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. GLASS ESTATE / SERVANT'S CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS

Valerie and Chloe skid across the slick hardwood. The narrow hallway chokes down to a single, heavy oak door. The basement entrance.

The wood bows outward. It bulges against the frame. Thick, black sludge drips from the brass hinges, pooling on the imported tile.

Chloe stares at the oozing liquid.

CHLOE
The structural integrity is completely compromised, standard load-bearing metrics don't account for--

VALERIE
It's just settling. Vintage charm.

Valerie slams her shoulder against the oak. The door refuses to budge.

VALERIE
Put your weight into it!

Chloe steps into the puddle of sludge. She throws her body against the wood alongside Valerie. 

A wet squelch echoes from inside the walls. The door holds fast.

CHLOE
I should update the vendor calendar, we need to schedule an emergency extraction team--

VALERIE
Forget the contractors! The handle!

Valerie backs up. She grabs Chloe by the blazer, yanking her back. 

VALERIE
On three. 

They sync their breathing. 

VALERIE
Three!

They lunge forward. Both women drive their heels directly into the brass knob.

Wood splinters. The strike plate tears free from the jamb. 

The bloated door violently gives way, spilling them forward into the pitch black.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / BASEMENT STAIRWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drags Chloe onto the top landing. She spots a narrow, outward-leading HVAC vent near the ceiling. Valerie hoists Chloe up by the waist, shoving the assistant into the metal duct. As Chloe scrambles out of sight, the wooden stairs beneath Valerie collapse, melting into a slick, acidic slide. Valerie plunges into the dark cellar.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The separation of mentor and mentee; Valerie taking on the final burden alone to ensure Chloe's survival. Micro-Conflict: (><) External: The collapsing stairs vs. Valerie's footing. Interpersonal: Valerie forcing Chloe to safety instead of letting her stay and fight. Value Shift: (+/-) From together (+) to isolated and falling (-).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. GLASS ESTATE / BASEMENT STAIRWAY - CONTINUOUS

Valerie hauls Chloe by the collar, throwing them both onto the narrow top landing. 

The mahogany walls groan. The floorboards ripple.

CHLOE
I cross-referenced the county blueprints, the foundational load-bearing beams shouldn't be shifting at this velocity, if we just reroute through the primary egress--

VALERIE
The primary egress is currently digesting the board of directors. 

Valerie scans the ceiling. Dust rains from the plaster. 

She spots a stamped-steel HVAC vent near the crown molding. Outward-leading.

Valerie rips the grate off. Screws ping against the shaking hardwood.

VALERIE
Up.

Valerie locks her hands together, forming a step. 

CHLOE
I still need to finalize the escrow transfers for tomorrow morning, I can't just leave you here with the compliance paperwork--

VALERIE
Your blazer. The tailoring is flawless. Keep it clean. Step on my hands!

Chloe hesitates. 

A four-color pen slips from Chloe's trembling fingers. It clatters onto the floorboards. 

The wood beneath the pen softens. The plastic melts into a puddle of toxic slag.

Chloe steps into Valerie's palms. 

Valerie grunts, hoisting the assistant upward. 

Chloe's shoulders scrape against the raw metal duct. She kicks, wedging herself into the tight shaft. 

CHLOE
Grab my hand! The tensile strength of this duct can support both of our weight metrics if we--

VALERIE
It's a single-occupancy unit. 

Valerie shoves Chloe's boots hard, forcing her deep into the ventilation shaft. 

Chloe slides out of sight. 

Beneath Valerie, the landing splinters. 

The polished oak stairs warp. The wood liquefies, bubbling into a slick, translucent sludge. Acid hisses against the baseboards, eating through the imported rugs.

Valerie backs against the peeling wallpaper. There is no floor left. 

She stares down the melting, frictionless chute leading into the pitch-black cellar. 

Her thumb twitches. She rubs it aggressively against her index finger, counting invisible hundred-dollar bills. Faster and faster.

The last solid plank snaps.

Valerie drops. 

She plunges down the acidic chute, swallowed by the dark.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. GLASS ESTATE / UTILITY ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie crashes onto the concrete floor. The room glows from a massive, beating furnace. She scrambles up, grabs a heavy iron pipe wrench from a workbench, and violently shears the main gas line. Toxic fumes hiss into the room. Thick, sentient insulation cables drop from the ceiling, wrapping tightly around her ankles and dragging her toward the roaring furnace flames. Valerie stops struggling. Her thumb obsessively rubs against her index finger one last time. She pulls a gold-plated lighter from her pocket, sparks the flame, mutters a quip about the housing bubble bursting, and tosses it into the gas cloud.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The climax of the film; the destruction of the entity and Valerie's ultimate sacrifice. Micro-Conflict: (><) External: The sentient cables dragging her vs. Valerie's grip on the lighter. Internal: Her old greed (represented by the gold lighter) vs. her new destructive resolve. Value Shift: (+/-) From restrained and doomed (-) to victorious and defiant (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. GLASS ESTATE / UTILITY ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Valerie slams hard onto the concrete floor. A cloud of drywall dust plumes around her.

She doesn't move. For a long moment, the only sound is the deep, rhythmic thud of the massive industrial furnace.

The cast-iron belly glows cherry red. It breathes.

Valerie groans. She spits a mouthful of blood onto the cement. 

She forces herself onto her hands and knees. Her torn blazer hangs off her shoulder.

She scans the dim utility room. Shadows dance against the brick walls. 

A heavy steel workbench sits bolted to the far corner.

Valerie drags herself to her feet. She leans against a support pillar, catching her breath. 

She limps toward the bench. Her ruined designer heels scrape a jagged rhythm against the floor.

Tools hang neatly on a pegboard. Claw hammers. Saws.

She bypasses them. Her eyes lock onto a three-foot iron pipe wrench.

She grabs the handle. It weighs twenty pounds. 

She drags the heavy iron head along the floor. It leaves a trail of orange sparks in her wake.

The furnace flares. The temperature in the room spikes twenty degrees. 

The house knows her intent. Overhead pipes groan under sudden pressure.

Valerie stops in front of a thick, yellow pipe running from floor to ceiling. 

Stenciled in faded black letters: MAIN GAS SUPPLY.

She hoists the massive wrench over her shoulder. Her muscles shake. 

She swings. 

Metal clangs against metal. The yellow pipe dents.

The floorboards above her shriek. The brick walls seem to shrink inward.

Valerie grunts. She swings again. Harder.

The iron wrench bites deep into the yellow pipe. A violent hiss erupts.

A high-pressure jet of raw gas sprays directly into her face.

She coughs, stumbling backward. The air instantly shimmers with toxic fumes.

Above her, the ceiling tiles buckle. Plaster rains down.

Thick, black fiberglass insulation cables burst through the ceiling. They writhe like blind snakes.

Valerie turns to run.

A cable whips down. It coils tightly around her left ankle.

She crashes face-first onto the concrete. The wrench clatters away into the dark.

Another cable drops. It wraps around her right leg, fibers digging through her pantyhose into her skin.

They yank her backward.

Valerie screams. She claws at the floor. 

Her manicured nails snap against the unforgiving cement.

The cables drag her mercilessly toward the furnace. 

The heavy iron doors grind open. Inside, a vortex of white-hot fire waits.

The heat singes her hair. Ten feet away.

She thrashes, kicking her legs wildly. The cables only tighten their grip.

Eight feet. 

She grabs a rusted floor drain, fingers hooking the grate.

A cable whips around her wrist. It snaps her arm back.

Five feet. The roaring flames reflect in her wide, terrified eyes.

Valerie stops kicking. Her chest heaves. 

Her body goes entirely limp.

She stares into the fire.

Her free right hand trembles. Her thumb presses hard against her index finger. 

She rubs them together. A frantic, obsessive rhythm. Counting invisible bills.

The cables drag her closer. Three feet. The heat blisters her cheek.

She reaches into her torn blazer pocket. 

Her fingers close around a cold, smooth rectangle.

She pulls out a heavy, gold-plated lighter.

The hissing gas pools thick around her face. The entire room is a powder keg.

She flips the lighter open with her thumb.

VALERIE
Market's over-saturated.

She coughs violently, choking on the thick fumes.

VALERIE
Time for a--

A cable wraps around her waist. It hoists her off the ground.

VALERIE
--a hard correction.

She strikes the flint. 

A tiny orange flame flickers to life.

Valerie smiles.

She tosses the lighter into the gas cloud.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. GLASS ESTATE - NIGHT
scenes.narrative_action: The massive modernist mansion violently detonates. A massive fireball shatters the reinforced glass windows, sending burning debris and black sludge raining down onto the manicured lawn. The structure collapses inward, entirely obliterated.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The visual confirmation of the monster's death. Micro-Conflict: (><) External: The explosive force vs. the structural integrity of the house. Value Shift: (+/-) From an imposing threat (-) to a pile of burning rubble (+).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. GLASS ESTATE - NIGHT

The modernist mansion sits against the dark skyline. Quiet. Still.

Then--

A blinding flash of orange light erupts from the core.

The structure detonates.

Reinforced glass windows blow outward in a million jagged shards. A roaring fireball consumes the geometric rooflines.

Burning debris rockets into the night sky.

It rains down across the manicured lawn. Thick, black sludge splatters alongside the embers, sizzling against the pristine grass.

The steel framework groans. The support beams buckle.

The entire mansion collapses inward. It swallows itself in a churning cloud of smoke and fire.

Nothing remains but a burning, jagged crater.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. STREET / AMBULANCE - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: A quiet aftermath. Valerie, covered in soot and sporting a fresh burn on her face, sits on the bumper of an ambulance next to Chloe. A paramedic wraps a thermal blanket around Valerie's shoulders. They stare in silence at the smoldering foundation of the estate. No words are spoken. They just breathe the cool night air.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The mandated Breather scene; allowing the characters and audience to process the fallout of the explosion before the epilogue. Micro-Conflict: (><) Internal: Valerie processing the loss of her wealth vs. the relief of her survival. Value Shift: (+/-) From adrenaline-fueled (+) to exhausted but safe (+).
scenes.draft_text:
EXT. STREET / AMBULANCE - LATER

Red and blue emergency lights sweep across the wet asphalt. 

In the distance, the Glass Estate is gone. A blackened, jagged crater smokes against the night sky. 

Fire hoses spray weak streams of water over the glowing embers. 

VALERIE sits on the reinforced steel bumper of a parked ambulance. 

Her tailored blazer is scorched into rags. A fresh, angry burn runs along her jawline. Her face is caked in heavy gray soot. 

CHLOE sits shoulder-to-shoulder with her. Her knees are pulled tight to her chest. 

A PARAMEDIC steps into frame. He shakes out a crinkling, silver thermal blanket and drapes it over Valerie’s shoulders. 

Valerie does not look up. She does not acknowledge him. 

The paramedic moves away, leaving the two women alone in the spinning lights. 

Valerie’s hands rest in her lap. Her thumb brushes her index finger. The old instinct to count invisible bills. 

She stops. She lets her hand fall flat against her thigh. Empty. 

Chloe turns her head, looking at the side of Valerie's face. 

Valerie keeps her eyes locked on the smoldering foundation. The grave of her empire. 

A cold wind rolls down the street, rustling the foil blanket. 

Valerie exhales. A long, slow breath that turns to white vapor in the chill air. 

Chloe leans her head against Valerie's shoulder. 

Valerie does not pull away. She closes her eyes and takes another deep breath of the cool night air. 

The emergency lights continue to spin in the quiet dark.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT OFFICE - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Six months later. The morning sun illuminates a dingy, fluorescent-lit office in a strip mall. Valerie sits behind a scratched laminate desk, wearing a simple cotton sweater, her face bearing a faint, permanent burn scar. She slides a set of keys across the table to a working-class family. Across the room, Chloe answers a ringing landline, calmly taking notes without a single click of her pen. A disgruntled tenant walks in, loudly complaining about a leaky faucet. Valerie looks up from her paperwork and smiles a genuine, unburdened smile.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The Final Image; proving Valerie's permanent moral transformation and the breaking of the cycle of greed. Micro-Conflict: (><) External: The mundane annoyance of a leaky faucet vs. Valerie's newfound inner peace. Value Shift: (+/-) From wealthy and soulless (-) to scarred, broke, and genuinely happy (+).
scenes.draft_text:
INT. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT OFFICE - DAY

SUPERIMPOSE: SIX MONTHS LATER

Fluorescent tubes hum above a water-stained ceiling. Faded posters of generic suburban homes cling to cheap wood paneling.

Valerie sits behind a scratched laminate desk. She wears a simple, oversized cotton sweater. 

A jagged, faded burn scar crawls up the left side of her neck, disappearing into her hairline.

Across the desk sits HECTOR, a man in a worn mechanic's jacket.

Valerie taps a printed lease agreement.

VALERIE
First month and last month are locked in escrow. 

Hector stares at the paperwork. He rubs his calloused hands together.

VALERIE
By state law, the property owner cannot touch those funds. They have exactly fourteen days to itemize any deductions upon move-out or they forfeit the right to charge you.

HECTOR
My last landlord just changed the locks. 

Valerie holds his gaze.

VALERIE
That won't happen here.

Valerie slides a pair of brass keys across the scratched laminate.

VALERIE
It's a high-yield neighborhood for community resources. Good transit access. You'll thrive there.

Hector takes the keys. He stands, offering a stiff nod, and exits the glass storefront door.

Across the room, Chloe pins a phone receiver between her ear and shoulder. She writes on a yellow legal pad.

CHLOE
According to municipal code section four-B we requested the HVAC inspection three weeks ago so if the contractor isn't onsite by noon I'm filing a formal grievance with the city housing authority and withholding the monthly disbursement.

Chloe clicks the receiver down. She sets her four-color pen on the desk. 

She doesn't click it.

The storefront door swings open, rattling the cheap brass bell. 

GARY, fifty and wearing a paint-splattered shirt, marches inside. He slams a damp towel onto Valerie’s desk.

GARY
The faucet.

Valerie looks at the towel.

GARY
Unit four. It’s dripping again. I put a bucket under the trap but the pipe is stripped. 

Valerie traces the frayed edge of the damp towel. 

GARY
I work nights. I can't sleep with it pinging against the plastic--

CHLOE
I have a plumber in the system who owes us a favor for a zoning variance so I can dispatch him by two o'clock.

GARY
I don't want him by two. I want it fixed now. 

Gary glares at Valerie. He leans over the desk, invading her space. 

GARY
You people are useless.

Valerie looks at Gary. She looks at the dingy walls. The scratched desk. The damp, dirty towel.

The corners of her mouth twitch. 

Valerie smiles. It reaches her eyes. A genuine, unburdened smile.

VALERIE
I'll grab my toolbox.

stage8_coverage.title: Open House
stage8_coverage.logline: Facing bankruptcy, a cutthroat luxury realtor discovers her newest listing is a sentient, flesh-eating mansion that excretes flawless diamonds, prompting her to feed it the city's worst elites until the house's insatiable appetite traps her inside.

synopsis.setup: Valerie, a ruthless and secretly bankrupt luxury realtor, is given a final chance to save her career by selling the stagnant Glass Estate. During a private showing, the brutalist mansion consumes a wealthy client and leaves behind a flawless diamond. Realizing the house's deadly but lucrative mechanics, Valerie strikes a dark pact with the entity, choosing to exploit the property for wealth rather than report the murder.
synopsis.escalation: Valerie begins luring the city's worst elites—including corrupt politicians and toxic tech bros—to the estate, trading their lives for diamonds. Her assistant Chloe discovers the truth just as Valerie's ruthless boss Richard forces a massive gala at the property. The house's hunger grows insatiable, locking the doors and indiscriminately attacking the guests as the architecture comes alive and shifts to trap them.
synopsis.resolution: As the mansion digests the attendees and shifts its floor plan, Valerie abandons her greed and sacrifices her diamond fortune to save Chloe. They navigate the fleshy, labyrinthine architecture to reach the basement, where Valerie ignites the gas main to destroy the estate. Six months later, a reformed Valerie works at a modest property management office with Chloe, finally free of her ruthless ambition.

red_flags: Repetitive micro-expressions used as emotional shorthand (Valerie rubbing thumb and index finger/counting invisible bills).
Overuse of 'SMASH CUT TO:' as a rhythmic crutch between scenes.
Precision-tooled archetype supporting characters that serve only as satirical fodder.
Repetitive dialogue crutches, specifically Valerie complimenting tailoring or clothing to deflect tension.

strengths.headline: Highly marketable, elevated concept
strengths.detail: The premise of a sentient house that eats the rich and produces diamonds is a brilliant satirical hook that perfectly aligns with the protagonist's financial desperation.

strengths.headline: Strong, active protagonist
strengths.detail: Valerie is a proactive lead whose journey from a cutthroat shark to a reformed survivor provides a satisfying and earned emotional arc.

strengths.headline: Inventive, architecture-based set pieces
strengths.detail: The house's methods of consumption—such as the acid pool and shifting fleshy walls—are creative, visually striking, and genre-appropriate.

weaknesses.headline: One-note, robotic supporting characters
weaknesses.detail: Richard and Chloe function as plot devices rather than people, with Richard speaking in architectural metaphors and Chloe in logistical run-on sentences.

weaknesses.headline: Repetitive character tics and writing loops
weaknesses.detail: Valerie’s physical tics and conversational deflections, like complimenting tailoring, are overused to the point of feeling algorithmic.

weaknesses.headline: Repetitive climax mechanics
weaknesses.detail: The third act relies too heavily on a loop of running through shifting hallways and narrow escapes, which stalls the narrative momentum.

macro_todo.task: Humanize Chloe and Richard by giving them natural dialogue that avoids pure exposition or constant architectural metaphors.
Condense and vary the Act III escape sequence to move beyond repetitive shifting walls and maintain a propulsive pace.
Differentiate the victims by giving them unique humanizing traits so they feel like more than just satirical punchlines.
Deepen Valerie's moral descent in Act II so her transition from desperate realtor to mass murderer feels earned rather than instantaneous.
Deepen the lore or mechanics of the house to provide a clearer reason for its escalation during the gala.

micro_todo.task: Severely limit the 'counting invisible bills' action line, using it only at critical moments of financial panic.
Remove the repetitive 'SMASH CUT TO:' transitions and let the scene pacing dictate the cuts.
Vary Valerie's deflection tactics beyond complimenting tailoring to make her manipulation feel less predictable.
Break up Chloe's run-on sentences with natural pauses and breaths to make her panic feel grounded.
Trim superlative-heavy and novelistic action lines to focus strictly on what can be seen and heard on screen.

recommendation.justification: The script features a highly marketable and inventive horror-comedy premise with a strong protagonist arc and creative set pieces. However, it requires a significant polish to humanize the robotic supporting cast, eliminate repetitive writing tics, and streamline the repetitive escape sequences in the final act.

working.1:
INT. VALERIE'S SEDAN - DAY

Tight on the rearview mirror. VALERIE (30s) stares back. Dark circles shadow her eyes. 

She drags a tube of crimson lipstick across her mouth. A harsh, precise slash of color.

The sedan's interior tells the real story. Dust coats the dashboard. A neon-yellow REPOSSESSION WARNING tag wedges out from the cracked glovebox.

Valerie snaps the lipstick cap shut. A sharp plastic crack.

She shoves the repo tag deeper into the glovebox. Slams the compartment shut. 

She looks back into the mirror. Inhales.

The exhaustion vanishes. She stretches her lips into a blinding, high-gloss smile. 

The armor is on.

SMASH CUT TO:
working.2:
EXT. STARTER HOME - CONTINUOUS

Stiletto heels spear through dandelions in a cracked concrete driveway. Valerie marches toward the porch.

Waiting on the steps: MARK and SARAH. Sarah squints up at the sagging, water-stained roof eaves.

VALERIE
Mid-century modern bones. You don't find structural integrity like this anymore.

Valerie inserts herself directly between Sarah and the roofline. 

SARAH
Is that water damage? The gutter looks--

VALERIE
Sarah, I have to say, that vintage denim jacket is incredible. It perfectly matches the bespoke aesthetic we're walking into. 

Valerie grips Mark's elbow, physically steering him toward the peeling front door. 

VALERIE
First-time buyers get paralyzed by cosmetics. We are looking at the canvas. The equity.

MARK
We just want to make sure the inspection--

VALERIE
--Will show a property that survived three decades of market fluctuation. 

She shoves a brass key into the deadbolt. Twists. 

VALERIE
Let's get you inside.

She pushes the heavy door open and actively herds them over the threshold.
working.3:
INT. STARTER HOME - CONTINUOUS

The living room is a cramped, low-ceilinged box. Water stains map the popcorn ceiling. 

TODD flares his nostrils, sniffing the stale, damp air. Beside him, JENNA pulls her wool coat tighter.

VALERIE glides smoothly into their path. She plants her heels directly in front of a sprawling, fuzzy patch of black mold crawling up the drywall.

VALERIE
You are looking at a thirty percent year-over-year equity spike. The urban revitalization initiatives here are aggressive.

Todd leans left, trying to see past Valerie's shoulder. 

Valerie mirrors his step. She completely blocks the rotting plaster.

VALERIE
Micro-breweries. Mixed-use zoning. In six months, this zip code will be entirely--

TODD
It smells like wet pennies in here.

Todd points a finger toward the baseboards.

TODD
And what is that dark stuff on the--

Valerie's phone violently vibrates against her hip. A harsh, mechanical buzz echoing in the empty room.

She shifts her weight. The buzzing continues.

JENNA
Do you need to get that?

Valerie glances down. Her screen is cracked down the middle. 

Through the splintered glass: CHASE BANK. FINAL OVERDRAFT NOTICE. BALANCE -$412.50.

Valerie grips the phone until her knuckles turn white. Her breathing stalls.

TODD
Is the foundation leaking? Because my brother bought a flip and--

Valerie forces a blinding, high-wattage smile. She steps straight toward Jenna, abandoning the mold-covered wall.

VALERIE
I have been trying to place the stitching on that lapel since we met in the driveway.

Jenna blinks, stepping back.

JENNA
My coat?

VALERIE
It is vintage Givenchy, isn't it? The drape is flawless.

Valerie lightly touches Jenna's sleeve. She physically turns the woman toward the hallway.

VALERIE
A garment like that requires an owner who understands long-term investment. Just like this property.

Valerie guides Jenna out of the room.

VALERIE
Let me show you the natural light in the primary bedroom. You will die when you see the crown molding.

Todd takes one last look at the exposed black mold. He frowns, then hurries after them.
working.4:
EXT. STARTER HOME - LATER

The YOUNG COUPLE speed-walks down the cracked driveway. The husband shakes his head. The wife refuses to look back.

Valerie stands on the rotting wooden porch. Her blinding, 100-watt realtor smile holds steady. 

The couple's Prius turns the corner and disappears. 

Valerie's smile vanishes. Her facial muscles sag. 

She pulls a cracked iPhone from her tailored blazer. The screen illuminates the fading daylight. 

CHASE BANK - OVERDRAFT ALERT: -$314.50. 

Valerie stares at the glowing red numbers. 

She locks the screen. Shoves the phone deep into her pocket. 

She marches off the porch. Her expensive heels sink into the dead lawn as she heads straight for a sun-faded, dust-covered Honda sedan.
working.5:
INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE / BREAKROOM - LATER

Sterile quartz countertops. Floor-to-ceiling glass walls. 

VALERIE (30s) aggressively scrubs a coffee stain out of her silk cuff with a damp paper towel. 

RICHARD (50s) fills the doorway. He doesn't lean. He just occupies the space.

RICHARD
A structure is only as resilient as its foundation, Valerie.

Valerie freezes. She tosses the paper towel into the brushed-steel bin, masking her surprise with a sharp smile.

VALERIE
Richard. I was just reviewing the Q3 analytics. We are seeing a massive upward trend in transitional neighborhoods--

RICHARD
When moisture breaches the concrete, the rebar rusts from the inside out. It isn't an accident.

Richard steps into the room. He approaches the espresso machine.

RICHARD
It is an inherent flaw in the material itself. A lack of structural integrity that spreads to everything it touches.

Valerie backs up slightly, her heel hitting the lower cabinets.

VALERIE
The bespoke tailoring on that suit is flawless, by the way. Milan?

Richard picks up a stray sugar packet from the counter. He places it next to another. He aligns their edges until they are perfectly parallel.

RICHARD
Do you know what we do when the rot takes hold?

VALERIE
I have three ultra-high-net-worth clients flying in from Dubai tomorrow. 

RICHARD
We demolish it. We clear the debris to protect the healthy investments.

Valerie stands perfectly still. 

Behind her back, her right hand drops. Her thumb rubs obsessively against her index finger. Counting invisible bills.

VALERIE
My portfolio is temporarily unoptimized. A minor liquidity friction. I just need a premier property to leverage.

RICHARD
What is the purpose of a luxury brokerage, Valerie?

VALERIE
To curate exclusive lifestyle assets for--

RICHARD
To act as a gatekeeper. To ensure the blight of the lower tiers does not infect the pinnacle. You are currently bringing blight into my office.

Valerie's thumb rubs her index finger hard enough to chafe the skin.

VALERIE
Give me the Sullivan penthouse. I can move it in forty-eight hours.

Richard reaches into his tailored pocket. He withdraws a ring of heavy, antique brass keys. 

He drops them onto the quartz counter. They hit with a heavy, dead clatter.

RICHARD
The Glass Estate.

Valerie stares at the keys. 

VALERIE
That property has been stagnant for two years. The previous agent--

RICHARD
Close it by the thirty-first. Or we clear the debris.

Richard turns and walks out, leaving the doorway empty.

Valerie remains pinned against the counter. She doesn't look at the door. She just stares at the brass keys.
working.6:
INT. VALERIE'S SEDAN - LATER

A luxury sedan tears around a blind hairpin turn. Tires shriek against the asphalt.

Behind the wheel, Valerie steers with one palm. A sheer cliff drop-off blurs past her passenger window. She never glances at the treacherous curves ahead.

Resting on the empty leather passenger seat: a set of heavy brass keys. They slide and clink against the upholstery with every aggressive swerve.

Valerie balances her phone on the top arc of the steering wheel. Her thumb rapidly taps a calculator app.

She punches in a massive base listing price: 35,000,000.

She multiplies it by 0.06.

Hits equals.

$2,100,000.

The sedan drifts over the double-yellow line. 

An oncoming horn BLARES. 

Valerie jerks the wheel hard. The sedan snaps back into the right lane, narrowly dodging a speeding flatbed truck. 

Her chest heaves. She stabilizes the steering wheel.

She doesn't put the phone down. Her eyes lock right back onto the seven-figure commission glowing in the dark cabin.
working.7:
EXT. THE GLASS ESTATE - DAY

Tires grind into white gravel. A sleek Mercedes SUV brakes hard near the edge of a jagged cliff.

VALERIE steps out. Her designer heels click against the loose stone. 

She looks up.

The Glass Estate looms. A brutalist slab of concrete and black glass, bolted directly into the rock face. It doesn't sit on the landscape. It digs in.

Valerie stands at the base of the wide stone steps, entirely dwarfed by the architecture. A rare flicker of hesitation crosses her face. 

She pulls a glossy listing folder from her bag, tapping it against her thigh. The hesitation vanishes, replaced by a calculated, hungry smirk.

She marches up the steps. Her pace is sharp, rhythmic, conquering the distance.

She reaches the oversized front door and retrieves a heavy brass key. 

She slides the metal into the deadbolt. 

Click.
working.8:
INT. THE GLASS ESTATE / FOYER - CONTINUOUS

The heavy oak door glides open. Valerie steps inside.

The foyer is a cavern of polished concrete and smoked glass. It echoes like a modern mausoleum. 

She runs a hand along the wall. Finds a sleek, brushed-steel switch. Flicks it up.

Recessed lighting hums to life. 

Beside the switch, a flush-mounted smart-home interface panel wakes up. A digital iris blinks on the screen. It pulses with a deep, crimson glow.

The red light bleeds across the floorboards. 

Valerie doesn't even glance at it. She snaps a clipboard from her leather tote bag. 

VALERIE
Let's maximize the footprint. We need to warm up this entryway.

She uncaps a pen with her teeth. Paces across the red-stained marble.

VALERIE
Too sterile. High-net-worth buyers want a sanctuary, not a tomb. We'll source some mid-century organic textures.

The crimson panel pulses faster. A low, sub-bass vibration hums through the floor. 

Valerie taps her pen against her chin, completely oblivious. 

VALERIE
And a statement rug. Something plush to soften the acoustics. 

She jots down a furious note, stepping deeper into the belly of the house.
working.9:
INT. THE GLASS ESTATE / HALLWAYS - CONTINUOUS

Valerie strides down the corridor. Her designer heels click against the imported Brazilian walnut in a rapid, military cadence.

Floor-to-ceiling glass panes line the left wall. They reflect her sharp silhouette against the fading afternoon light.

The hallway stretches unnaturally long. Symmetrical. Sterile.

Thump.

A deep vibration ripples through the floorboards.

Valerie halts. Her right heel stays planted.

Thump.

The wood grain shivers beneath her sole. A low, rhythmic pulse. It mimics a heavy bass from a distant stereo, but it rises directly from the foundation.

She stares down at the pristine hardwood. Her brow furrows.

She shifts her weight. The pulse thrums against the arch of her foot. Steady.

VALERIE
Three percent of forty million. One point two.

She checks her reflection in the glass pane. She straightens her blazer lapel.

VALERIE
Faulty HVAC. Deduct ten grand for the boiler inspection. Still clears a million.

She shakes her head, physically waving away the anomaly.

Valerie resumes her march. She strikes the floorboards harder now, forcing the sharp clack of her heels to drown out the heartbeat.

She turns the corner, descending deeper into the house.
working.10:
INT. THE GLASS ESTATE / KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS

Valerie strides into the cavernous kitchen. She sweeps a hand over the massive waterfall-edge island.

VALERIE
Bespoke Italian cabinetry. Fully integrated smart appliances. You are stepping into a curated lifestyle--

INVESTOR
It’s cold.

The Investor trails behind her. He raps his knuckles against the marble.

INVESTOR
Feels like a clinic.

Valerie forces a tight, practiced smile. She taps her pen against her plastic clipboard.

VALERIE
The architect prioritized an open-concept flow. It guarantees an unprecedented ROI once the neighborhood--

INVESTOR
I don't care about the flow. I care about the bones.

He drags the toe of his designer loafer across the floorboards.

INVESTOR
New builds settle. They crack. 

VALERIE
The engineering is flawless. And I have to say, that is a stunning Patek on your wrist. The craftsmanship--

INVESTOR
Stop pitching.

He steps into the center of the room.

INVESTOR
Listen to this.

He stomps his loafer hard against the oak.

INVESTOR
Hear that give?

Valerie steps forward, extending a placating hand.

VALERIE
Sir, the developer sourced premium--

INVESTOR
It's hollow!

He stomps again. He puts his full weight into the heel.

The wood ripples.

The solid oak beneath his feet liquefies.

A gaping, fleshy maw tears open in the floor.

Jagged, calcified ridges line the wet throat.

The Investor drops waist-deep into the tissue.

INVESTOR
What the--

The maw snaps shut.

A wet, heavy crunch echoes off the marble.

The floorboards instantly snap back into place.

Pristine oak. Seamless.

Not a single drop of blood remains.

Valerie stands entirely paralyzed. 

Her fingers go slack.

The plastic clipboard hits the hardwood.

It shatters into pieces.
working.11:
INT. THE GLASS ESTATE / KITCHEN - MORNING

Morning sunlight slices across an ocean of white marble. 

VALERIE stands at the center island. She slides a stack of heavy, high-gloss brochures across the stone. 

She aligns the corners precisely with the edge of the counter. 

VALERIE
(to the empty room)
Open-concept living. Unmatched natural light. A sanctuary for the modern visionary.

She stops. Her shoulders drop. She leans her weight against the cold marble.

Her phone vibrates on the counter. A banking alert flashes on the lock screen. 

ACCOUNT OVERDRAWN. 

Valerie swipes the notification away before it fully renders. She shoves the phone into her blazer pocket.

She crosses to the massive Sub-Zero refrigerator. 

She stares at her distorted reflection in the stainless steel door. Dark circles shadow her eyes. 

VALERIE
You aren't just acquiring a property. You are securing an architectural legacy. 

Her voice is entirely flat. The pitch sounds hollow in the cavernous room.

She presses her fingertips to her cheekbones. She pushes her skin upward, forcing a blinding, high-wattage smile. 

The customer-service mask locks into place. Her eyes remain dead.

THUD. THUD. THUD.

A heavy, impatient knock echoes from the front door. 

Valerie flinches. Her thumb twitches, rubbing aggressively against her index finger as if counting invisible bills. 

She snatches a brochure off the island. 

The smile returns to her face, sharp as glass. She pivots and marches toward the foyer.
working.12:
INT. THE GLASS ESTATE / FOYER AND HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS

The heavy oak front door swings inward.

Before VALERIE can step back, a TECH INVESTOR (30s) shoulders past her. He wears a razor-sharp bespoke suit and custom leather loafers.

He stops dead in the center of the foyer. He cranes his neck, glaring at the ceiling.

TECH INVESTOR
Fourteen feet? The listing explicitly said vaulted.

VALERIE
It’s a fourteen-foot baseline with a recessed--

TECH INVESTOR
It’s a shoebox. I can practically touch the drywall.

He pivots, nearly clipping her shoulder. Valerie holds up a glossy, silver-embossed brochure.

VALERIE
If you review the floor plan, the spatial optimization--

He backhands the brochure. It smacks against the wall and flutters to the marble floor.

Valerie freezes. Her smile tightens. She steps back against the entryway console, physically boxed in by his aggressive posture.

TECH INVESTOR
I don’t read marketing copy. I read margins.

Valerie’s eyes dart from the fallen brochure to his chest. She straightens her posture.

VALERIE
Is that a Zegna weave?

He blinks, momentarily thrown.

VALERIE
The suit. It drapes flawlessly. And the Santoni loafers--such an elegant profile. You clearly appreciate high-net-worth craftsmanship.

The Investor scoffs, ignoring the praise. He turns his back on her and marches down the main corridor.

TECH INVESTOR
Craftsmanship? Look at this wainscoting.

Valerie hurries after him. She leaves the brochure on the floor.

TECH INVESTOR
It lacks sheen. Matte finish is for suburban tract housing. I told my people no domestic materials.

He runs a finger along the dark oak trim. He inspects his fingertip for dust.

VALERIE
You’re entirely right. It’s understated. Perhaps too subdued for a portfolio of your caliber.

TECH INVESTOR
It’s cheap.

VALERIE
Cost-effective aesthetics. But the foundation is prime equity. We can gut the paneling tomorrow.

He continues down the hall, his loafers clicking sharply against the floorboards. Valerie stays two steps behind.

TECH INVESTOR
I’m not buying a fixer-upper. I’m buying a turnkey asset to park capital.

VALERIE
And the return on investment here is unprecedented. The square footage alone--

TECH INVESTOR
Square footage doesn't matter if it looks like a dentist's waiting room.

VALERIE
Let me show you the chef's kitchen. The imported marble--

TECH INVESTOR
If the appliances aren't German, I'm walking.

VALERIE
Direct from Munich. Right this way.

She extends her arm, gesturing toward the archway ahead. Her hand trembles slightly. She quickly balls it into a fist.

He doesn't look at her. He just strides past, moving deeper into the house.

Valerie exhales a slow, shaky breath. She falls into step behind him.

working.14:
EXT. THE GLASS ESTATE / DRIVEWAY - CONTINUOUS

The heavy mahogany front doors blast open. Valerie explodes out of the mansion.

She kicks off her designer heels. Sprints barefoot across the manicured, razor-sharp Bermuda grass. 

Her breath comes in jagged, uneven gasps. She clutches her blazer tight against her chest, eyes locked on the exit.

A beat-up, repossessed sedan sits at the far end of the sweeping circular driveway. A rusting eyesore against the pristine modernist architecture.

Valerie reaches the driver's side. Her trembling hands fumble with the cheap plastic handle. 

She yanks it open, throws her entire body inside, and collapses across the driver's seat.

The metal door slams shut, sealing her in.

SMASH CUT TO:
working.15:
INT. VALERIE'S SEDAN - CONTINUOUS

The driver's door slams shut. Valerie strikes the master lock with the heel of her hand.

The locks engage with a heavy thud.

She grips the steering wheel. Her chest heaves. Her tailored blazer restricts her jagged breathing.

VALERIE
(muttering)
Standard procedure. Unforeseen... structural hazard.

She digs into her pocket and yanks out her phone.

Her hands tremble. She taps 9. Then 1. Then 1.

The glowing green CALL button illuminates her pale face.

She stares through the windshield. The modernist mansion sits on the hill. The massive glass windows reflect the fading sun. Pristine. Quiet.

VALERIE
Yes, Detective. I was conducting a private showing. Just Mr. Sterling and myself. And then the primary suite...

She chokes on the words.

VALERIE
The house ate him.

She drops her forehead against the steering wheel.

VALERIE
They'll audit my accounts.

She shifts her gaze to the passenger seat. A stack of mail spills from her open leather briefcase. Bold red ink stains the envelopes: FINAL NOTICE. FORECLOSURE WARNING.

A bankrupt realtor. A missing billionaire. No body. No witnesses.

VALERIE
Motive. Opportunity. I'm the--

She looks back at the mansion. The front door remains wide open. A dark void waiting in the marble foyer.

Her thumb hovers over the green button.

VALERIE
I didn't touch him. I didn't do anything.

The phone screen dims.

Valerie taps the glass. The emergency numbers stare back at her.

She presses the backspace key.

The second 1 vanishes.

She presses it again.

The first 1 vanishes.

She holds the backspace down. The screen clears entirely.

Valerie drops the phone into the center console. She shifts the car into drive.
working.16:
INT. THE GLASS ESTATE / KITCHEN - MOMENTS LATER

Valerie slips through the sliding glass door. The silence in the mansion rings in her ears. No screams. No sirens. Just the low hum of the smart-refrigerator.

She hugs the plaster wall, keeping her distance from the center of the room. She glances down at her designer heels. Too loud. She kicks them off.

Barefoot, she inches toward the marble kitchen island.

She nears the exact spot where the Investor stood just minutes ago. She braces herself, shoulders tight. Expecting a mess. Ruin. A crime scene.

She looks down. The seamless oak floor is spotless.

A beam of afternoon sunlight cuts across the room. It catches something resting near the base of the island.

A stone. The size of a human fist.

Valerie drops to her knees. Her breath hitches. She reaches out, her hands shaking violently.

Her manicured fingertips brush the faceted edge. It is freezing cold.

She scoops it up. The sheer density of the gem pulls her wrists down.

A flawless, rough-cut diamond.

Valerie stares into the refracted light bouncing off the stone and onto her pale face. The terror in her eyes slowly drains away.

She pulls the heavy jewel to her chest. Her free hand rises. She rubs her thumb against her index finger, counting out invisible bills. 

The trembling stops.

VALERIE
(whispering)
...equity.
working.17:
EXT. CITY STREET / ALLEYWAY - LATER

A polished sedan scrapes against the curb of a crumbling storefront. 

VALERIE steps out. Her tailored blazer looks entirely alien against the grime of the pavement. 

She pulls a heavy canvas tote bag from the passenger seat. The thick fabric sags, weighed down by the impossible harvest inside. 

She tucks the tote tightly against her ribs and walks fast. 

She turns into a narrow, unlit alleyway. Broken glass crunches under her expensive heels. 

A rusted fire escape rattles overhead in the wind. 

Valerie quickens her pace. Her knuckles turn white around the canvas straps. 

She stops at a dead end. A windowless, reinforced steel door waits in the decaying brick. 

She glances over her shoulder, then raises a fist to knock.
working.18:
INT. JULIAN'S FENCING OPERATION / FRONT STORE - CONTINUOUS

Fluorescent tubes buzz over cracked glass cases stuffed with pawned laptops and dusty power tools. 

Valerie pushes through the front door. Her pristine white blazer practically glows in the dingy, low-lit room. 

She ignores the cheap merchandise. Marches straight to the back wall.

A HULKING GUARD sits on a folding stool. Thick arms crossed over a tactical vest. He blocks a reinforced steel door.

Valerie halts. Her chest rises and falls in quick, shallow bursts. 

Her hand grips the strap of her leather purse. Knuckles turning bone-white.

She swallows hard. Forces her shoulders down. She locks eyes with the Guard. 

She doesn't blink.

The Guard stares back. A heavy, silent standoff. 

He breaks first. Reaches to his belt. 

Keys jingle. A heavy metallic THUNK echoes through the room as the deadbolt turns.

The steel door swings open, exhaling a draft of stale air. A narrow, unlit concrete corridor waits on the other side.

Valerie tightens her grip on her purse. She steps into the dark.
working.19:
INT. JULIAN'S FENCING OPERATION / BACK ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Fluorescent bulbs hum above a heavy, black velvet-lined table. A digital jeweler's scale sits in the center, glowing with a green zero.

Valerie yanks the zipper of her leather tote bag. She digs past pristine property brochures, her movements rigid and fast. She pulls out a bundle of heavy microfiber cloth.

She drops it onto the velvet.

VALERIE
Highly motivated seller. Turnkey transaction. I require immediate liquidity.

Julian leans over the table. He peels back the microfiber folds with the tip of a silver pen.

The diamond catches the harsh overhead light. It scatters a spectrum of colors across Julian's tailored silk shirt.

JULIAN
Good God, sweetheart.

Julian drops the pen. 

JULIAN
Look at her. Truly flawless stone.

VALERIE
The timeline is aggressive. My portfolio requires restructuring. Today.

Julian throws his head back. He laughs uproariously, the sound bouncing off the concrete walls. He wipes a tear from his eye.

JULIAN
Brilliant delivery, darling. Such a comedian.

VALERIE
I am discussing a time-sensitive transfer of assets.

JULIAN
Always the performer. Love the urgency.

Julian slides a jeweler's loupe over his right eye. He picks up the diamond with a pair of steel tweezers. He holds it inches from the bulb, turning it by millimeter increments.

VALERIE
Is there a market? I need a buyer who waives contingencies.

JULIAN
No carbon inclusions. Perfect light refraction. Uncharted territory, sweetheart.

Julian lowers the tweezers. He places the diamond onto the digital scale. 

The green numbers climb rapidly. They flash and lock into place.

Julian stares at the readout. His hand trembles slightly. 

He pulls a silk handkerchief from his breast pocket. He wraps it around his left hand and aggressively polishes his own gold rings, rubbing the metal until his knuckles turn white.

VALERIE
That pocket square. Mulberry silk? The hand-rolled edges are immaculate, Julian. Really elevates the lapel. Now, the valuation?

Julian doesn't look up from his rings. He keeps rubbing the gold.

JULIAN
Two million flat.

Valerie stops breathing. Her fingers tighten around the leather straps of her tote bag.

SUPERIMPOSE: SIX MONTHS LATER

Fluorescent tubes hum above a water-stained ceiling. Faded posters of generic suburban homes cling to cheap wood paneling.

Valerie sits behind a scratched laminate desk. She wears a simple, oversized cotton sweater. 

A jagged, faded burn scar crawls up the left side of her neck, disappearing into her hairline.

Across the desk sits HECTOR, a man in a worn mechanic's jacket.

Valerie taps a printed lease agreement.

VALERIE
First month and last month are locked in escrow. 

Hector stares at the paperwork. He rubs his calloused hands together.

VALERIE
By state law, the property owner cannot touch those funds. They have exactly fourteen days to itemize any deductions upon move-out or they forfeit the right to charge you.

HECTOR
My last landlord just changed the locks. 

Valerie holds his gaze.

VALERIE
That won't happen here.

Valerie slides a pair of brass keys across the scratched laminate.

VALERIE
It's a high-yield neighborhood for community resources. Good transit access. You'll thrive there.

Hector takes the keys. He stands, offering a stiff nod, and exits the glass storefront door.

Across the room, Chloe pins a phone receiver between her ear and shoulder. She writes on a yellow legal pad.

CHLOE
According to municipal code section four-B we requested the HVAC inspection three weeks ago so if the contractor isn't onsite by noon I'm filing a formal grievance with the city housing authority and withholding the monthly disbursement.

Chloe clicks the receiver down. She sets her four-color pen on the desk. 

She doesn't click it.

The storefront door swings open, rattling the cheap brass bell. 

GARY, fifty and wearing a paint-splattered shirt, marches inside. He slams a damp towel onto Valerie’s desk.

GARY
The faucet.

Valerie looks at the towel.

GARY
Unit four. It’s dripping again. I put a bucket under the trap but the pipe is stripped. 

Valerie traces the frayed edge of the damp towel. 

GARY
I work nights. I can't sleep with it pinging against the plastic--

CHLOE
I have a plumber in the system who owes us a favor for a zoning variance so I can dispatch him by two o'clock.

GARY
I don't want him by two. I want it fixed now. 

Gary glares at Valerie. He leans over the desk, invading her space. 

GARY
You people are useless.

Valerie looks at Gary. She looks at the dingy walls. The scratched desk. The damp, dirty towel.

The corners of her mouth twitch. 

Valerie smiles. It reaches her eyes. A genuine, unburdened smile.

VALERIE
I'll grab my toolbox.
